jobs.title: Territory Sales Manager (f/m/d)
jobs.description: The opportunity We are looking for a Territory Sales Manager to represent our factory's sales and marketing of High Voltage Bushings and On-Load Tap-Changers used in Transformers produced in our Swiss and Swedish components factories. In this role you will be part of a Sales team and develop an in-depth knowledge of our products and in combination with high sensitivity to our customers' needs you will find the right solutions to win their trust and thereby orders. You will be a part of a team that is passionate about continuous and quality assurance. We enjoy what we do, and our work has a direct impact on our customers, colleagues, and the company's success! This position is based in Altstetten, Zurich, Switzerland, and will require travel of around 20–30%. Your responsibilities Work with dedicated markets and generate new business by working closely with our customers to become their first choice for On-Load Tap-Changers and High Voltage Bushings for their transformers Establish and maintain strong customer relationships, including with transformer manufacturers and end users, who are often utility companies or large industries Follow up on sales opportunities to provide the best possible forecasts Cooperate with the Offer Engineering Department and Tendering Specialists when a customized solution is required. Comfortable interacting with other departments to close out projects Prepare technical\/commercial offers by translating customer requirements into appropriate technology solutions and proposals Follow the sales processes to ensure that customer requirements and contracts are effectively handed over to the project management team Establish plans and continuously follow up to ensure that both our short-term and long-term actions are executed as planned How you´ll make an impact We are looking for an engaged, determined and service-oriented individual who can work independently and as part of a team You have a technical background (an electrical and\/or mechanical degree), combined with a commercial attitude and expertise You have good communication, negotiation and presentation skills You are structured, persistent, well organized and committed to customer satisfaction Proven ability to develop and manage customer relationships You are a curious person with the drive to challenge yourself and continuously develop Fluency in English and intermediate knowledge of German (written and verbal) is required. Knowledge of other languages would be advantageous More about us You will do enriching work, as part of a dynamic, passionate, and collaborative team which is committed to innovation and continuous improvement You will be stretched, encouraged, and supported to grow – we pride ourselves on providing strong learning and development opportunities We offer an environment that fosters collaboration, connection, and diversity of thought Our culture is one of a kind: Come as you are and bring your unique attributes, passion and drive to Hitachi Energy, contributing to making us ‘the place to be' and to our purpose of advancing a sustainable energy future for all Flexible working, including hybrid\/remote working opportunities A competitive compensation and benefits package

jobs.title: R&D and Production Engineering Specialist 80 - 100% (f/m/d)
jobs.description: The opportunity Join our dynamic Business R&D team in Turgi, where we develop cutting-edge medium-voltage frequency converters and HVDC subsystems. You'll work in a collaborative environment with six power electronics engineers and three mechanical engineers, contributing to innovative solutions that have a global impact. This position combines 60% R&D and 40% production engineering, offering flexibility for research activities and hands-on involvement in manufacturing processes. How you'll make an impact • Research & Development (60%) Drive innovation in medium-voltage converter design and technology Develop and build from prototype until the final product Collaborate with global teams in Poland, Sweden, India and China Work arrangement: Flexible (remote or on-site as needed) • Production Engineering (40%) Ensure seamless integration of designs into manufacturing processes Optimize production workflows for efficiency and quality Provide technical support to production teams and troubleshoot issues Work arrangement: On-site presence required at our factories in Turgi and Seon Lead & implement lean management and CIP methodology Your background Degree in Mechanical Engineering or related field Experience in power electronics and converter technology Experience in mechanical design and development, with tools like SolidEdge, NX, SAP Knowledge of production processes and industrialization Strong analytical and problem-solving skills Excellent communication and teamwork abilities Fluent in English and German What We Offer Our flexible working practices help you optimize personal and business performance while creating an environment where all employees can develop their skills and grow. We also offer you: Diversity: Promotion of diversity and inclusion and cultural offers Numerous exciting career and training opportunities within Hitachi Energy Discounted conditions in our daycare centres in Baden and Oerlikon Extra days off between Christmas and New Year Subsidized meals at locations with canteens Discounts on memberships at various fitness centers Additional perks such as credit cards, petrol benefits, and more Additional perks such as credit cards, petrol benefits, and more

jobs.title: Test Engineer 80 - 100% (f/m/d)
jobs.description: Die Möglichkeit Du möchtest die Zukunft der Energie mitgestalten? In unserem Hochstromlabor arbeitest du an der Spitze technologischer Entwicklungen und trägst dazu bei, unsere Produkte sicherer und leistungsfähiger zu machen. Als Test Engineer bist du Teil eines engagierten Teams, das Prüfprozesse kontinuierlich weiterentwickelt und höchste Qualitätsstandards sicherstellt. Das kannst du bewirken Planung von Prüfungen und Ressourcen sowie Definition von Prüfprogrammen und Spezifikationen unter Berücksichtigung aktueller internationaler Normen und kundenspezifischer Anforderungen Auslegung und Überprüfung von Prüfkreisen und Versuchsanordnungen sowie Auswahl geeigneter Messmittel Durchführung von Entwicklungs- und Typprüfungen im Hochstromlabor (thermisch und dielektrisch) Sorgfältige und vollständige Dokumentation der Prüfergebnisse Zeitnahe und effiziente Erstellung von Prüfberichten Erfassung und Dokumentation der verrechenbaren Laborleistungen Kontinuierliche Optimierung von Prüf- und Arbeitsprozessen im Team Kostenkalkulation von Prüfungen und Laborleistungen sowie Erstellung von Angeboten für internationale Kunden Das bringst du mit Abgeschlossenes Studium in Elektro- oder Energietechnik (FH oder Universität) oder eine vergleichbare Ausbildung Erste Berufserfahrung im Fachbereich von Vorteil Kenntnisse im Umgang mit verschiedenen Messgeräten und -verfahren Vorzugsweise Kenntnisse im Bereich elektrischer Kontakte Interesse an der kontinuierlichen Weiterentwicklung von Laborausrüstung und Prüfprozessen Ausgeprägte Serviceorientierung mit Fokus auf Qualität und Effizienz Persönlichkeit mit guten organisatorischen Fähigkeiten Teamfähigkeit und hohe soziale Kompetenz Sehr gute Deutsch- und Englischkenntnisse in Wort und Schrift Mehr über uns Bei Hitachi Energy setzen wir auf Zusammenarbeit, Vielfalt und Innovation. Unsere Kultur fördert den offenen Austausch von Ideen, unterstützt individuelle Entwicklung und schafft ein Umfeld, in dem unterschiedliche Perspektiven geschätzt und integriert werden. Gemeinsam gestalten wir eine nachhaltige Energiezukunft.

Das kannst du bewirken Planung von Prüfungen und Ressourcen sowie Definition von Prüfprogrammen und Spezifikationen unter Berücksichtigung aktueller internationaler Normen und kundenspezifischer Anforderungen Auslegung und Überprüfung von Prüfkreisen und Versuchsanordnungen sowie Auswahl geeigneter Messmittel Durchführung von Entwicklungs- und Typprüfungen im Hochstromlabor (thermisch und dielektrisch) Sorgfältige und vollständige Dokumentation der Prüfergebnisse Zeitnahe und effiziente Erstellung von Prüfberichten Erfassung und Dokumentation der verrechenbaren Laborleistungen Kontinuierliche Optimierung von Prüf- und Arbeitsprozessen im Team Kostenkalkulation von Prüfungen und Laborleistungen sowie Erstellung von Angeboten für internationale Kunden Das bringst du mit Abgeschlossenes Studium in Elektro- oder Energietechnik (FH oder Universität) oder eine vergleichbare Ausbildung Erste Berufserfahrung im Fachbereich von Vorteil Kenntnisse im Umgang mit verschiedenen Messgeräten und -verfahren Vorzugsweise Kenntnisse im Bereich elektrischer Kontakte Interesse an der kontinuierlichen Weiterentwicklung von Laborausrüstung und Prüfprozessen Ausgeprägte Serviceorientierung mit Fokus auf Qualität und Effizienz Persönlichkeit mit guten organisatorischen Fähigkeiten Teamfähigkeit und hohe soziale Kompetenz Sehr gute Deutsch- und Englischkenntnisse in Wort und Schrift Mehr über uns Bei Hitachi Energy setzen wir auf Zusammenarbeit, Vielfalt und Innovation. Unsere Kultur fördert den offenen Austausch von Ideen, unterstützt individuelle Entwicklung und schafft ein Umfeld, in dem unterschiedliche Perspektiven geschätzt und integriert werden. Gemeinsam gestalten wir eine nachhaltige Energiezukunft.

jobs.title: Global Sustainability Market Engagement Manager
jobs.description: The Global Sustainability Market Engagement Manager enables the organisation to systematically anticipate, structure, and respond to sustainability‑related customer and stakeholder expectations . The role supports the transition from reactive, ad‑hoc handling of sustainability requests to a proactive, structured, and insight‑driven approach , by developing shared response processes, facilitating cross‑functional alignment, and translating external market signals into strategic sustainability intelligence. Key Responsibilities 1. Market & Stakeholder Sustainability Intelligence Capture, consolidate, and structure sustainability‑related expectations, requirements, and emerging topics from customers and other relevant stakeholders (e.g. regulators, investors, rating agencies). Identify recurring themes, trends, and early signals with potential relevance for market access, competitiveness, and organisational risk. Maintain a transparent and evolving overview of sustainability topics that are material or increasingly relevant from a market and stakeholder perspective. 2. Sustainability Request & Response Process Development Develop, implement, and continuously improve a structured process for managing sustainability‑related customer and stakeholder requests, in collaboration with relevant internal functions. Clarify intake, coordination, prioritisation, escalation, and response pathways to ensure consistency, efficiency, and clarity. Support definition of interfaces, roles, and ways of working across Sustainability, Marketing & Sales, Portfolio, Product Management, Supply Chain Management, Legal, and other functions, while respecting existing governance and decision rights. 3. Enable Consistent and Proactive Market Engagement Support the development of guidance, reference materials, and response frameworks for recurring sustainability topics. Help shift the organisation from case‑specific responses to anticipating and preparing for sustainability expectations likely to arise in future customer engagements. Facilitate alignment on external sustainability messaging and positioning across regions and businesses. 4. Strategic Insight & Material Topic Input Synthesise market‑derived sustainability insights and provide structured input to support: Sustainability strategy discussions Identification and prioritisation of material sustainability topics Portfolio, offering, and capability development considerations Contribute fact‑based insights on how external sustainability expectations evolve and what this implies for the organisation. 5. Cross‑Functional Collaboration & Enablement Act as a connecting interface between the Sustainability function and customer‑facing as well as enabling functions. Support Marketing & Sales and account teams with sustainability insights and background, particularly in strategic or complex customer contexts. Facilitate collaboration and alignment without assuming ownership of commercial, portfolio, or contractual decisions. Qualifications & Experience 5–10 years of relevant experience in sustainability, strategy, market engagement, or related business functions Strong understanding of sustainability topics relevant to industrial and energy‑related markets Proven experience working across functions and regions within a complex, global organization Ability to structure unstructured inputs and translate them into clear, actionable insights Strong facilitation, stakeholder engagement, and communication skills

jobs.title: Global SCM Training Leader, BU Transformers (f/m/d)
jobs.description: The opportunity Hitachi Energy is seeking a Global SCM Training Leader for the Business Unit Transformers to join our global training and supply chain community. In this role, you will define and drive the strategic direction, development, and deployment of Supply Chain Management (SCM) training programs across the business unit. You will play a key role in strengthening supply chain capabilities, enabling business performance, and supporting standardization, transformation, and continuous improvement across regions and functions. This is a unique opportunity to shape the future of SCM capability development, work closely with global and regional stakeholders, and contribute to innovate, inspire and deliver transformative results in forward-thinking supply chain organization. How you`ll make an impact Define and implement the SCM training strategy and roadmap aligned with Business Unit supply chain priorities and transformation initiatives Design, develop, and deploy global SCM training programs, ensuring consistency, scalability, and operational relevance for global, regional and factory teams Establish specific KPIs to measure effectiveness, impact, and continuous improvement of learning initiatives in line with Hitachi Energy SCM functional and BU Training governance Collaborate with SCM leaders, Subject Matter Experts, and global\/regional stakeholders to identify capability gaps and ensure technical accuracy of training content Support the adoption of standardized SCM processes, tools, and best practices through structured learning solutions Drive a culture of continuous learning and knowledge sharing across the SCM community Promote innovative learning approaches and digital solutions to enhance engagement and learning effectiveness Your background Degree in Supply Chain Management, Engineering, Business Administration, or a related field Minimum of 10 years of experience in Supply Chain Management, training, learning & development, or a related functional leadership role Proven experience in designing, deploying, and governing global training programs within complex, matrix organizations Strong understanding of end‑to‑end SCM processes, including sourcing, planning, logistics, supplier management, and sustainability Excellent stakeholder management and communication skills, with the ability to collaborate across global and regional teams Analytical mindset with experience in defining KPIs, measuring training impact, and driving continuous improvement Digital and technology savviness in managing and executing training using modern digital and AI-enabled technologies Ability to work effectively in an international environment and manage multiple projects\/initiatives in parallel with proven track record of successfully managed projects

jobs.title: Senior Key Account Manager Schweiz (w/m/d)
jobs.description: Die Möglichkeit Als Senior Key Account Manager übernehmen Sie die Verantwortung für einen strategisch bedeutenden Kunden im Bereich der Schweizer Energieinfrastruktur. Sie agieren dabei in einem anspruchsvollen, technisch geprägten Umfeld und arbeiten eng mit internen Fachbereichen zusammen, um nachhaltige Lösungen und langfristige Partnerschaften zu entwickeln. Der Schwerpunkt dieser Rolle liegt in der operativen und strategischen Betreuung des Kunden inklusive der aktiven Führung von Ausschreibungen und Angebotsprozessen.Sie fungieren als zentrale Schnittstelle zwischen Kunde und Hitachi Energy und stellen eine professionelle Zusammenarbeit auf allen relevanten Ebenen sicher. Das können Sie bewirken Gesamtverantwortung für einen strategischen Key Account im Bereich Netzinfrastrukur (Auftragseingang, Profitabilität, Kundenzufriedenheit) Aufbau und Pflege langfristiger, belastbarer Beziehungen zu relevanten Stakeholdern des Kunden auf allen Hierarchieebenen inklusive C-Level Sicherstellung eines konsistenten, professionellen Auftritts von Hitachi Energy gegenüber einem zentralen Akteur der Schweizer Energieversorgung Aktive Mitarbeit sowie fachliche Führung lokaler Ausschreibungen (Tenders) – von der Angebotsstrategie über die Verhandlungsphase bis zum Vertragsabschluss Entwicklung und Umsetzung einer kundenspezifischen Account‑ und Wachstumsstrategie (Marktpositionierung, Pipeline, Forecast, Wettbewerbsumfeld) Enge Zusammenarbeit mit internen Business Units und Funktionen (Sales, Engineering, Projektmanagement, Service etc.) zur optimalen Lösungs‑ und Angebotsgestaltung Anwendung und Weiterentwicklung standardisierter Sales‑ und CRM‑Prozesse und ‑Tools (z. B. Salesforce, Account Pläne, KPIs) Ihr Hintergrund Abgeschlossenes Hochschulstudium im Bereich Ingenieurwesen oder eine vergleichbare Qualifikation. Ergänzende Managementqualifikation wünschenswert Langjährige Erfahrung im Key Account Management oder im Vertrieb komplexer technischer Lösungen, idealerweise in den Bereichen Energieerzeugung, Übertragungs‑ und Verteilnetze, Energieversorgungsunternehmen oder Rechenzentren. Kenntnisse des Produktportfolios von Hitachi Energy sind wünschenswert. Nachgewiesene Erfahrung im Management komplexer Kundenbeziehungen sowie in der Entwicklung und Umsetzung von Vertriebsstrategien. Versiert im erfogreichen Manövrieren in den Strukturen eines Grosskonzerns und Marktführers Fundierte Erfahrung in der Bearbeitung und Führung von komplexen Ausschreibungen Fähigkeit, operativ detailnah zu arbeiten und gleichzeitig auf Top‑Management‑Ebene sicher aufzutreten Sehr gute Kenntnisse gängiger Sales‑, CRM‑ und Office‑Tools Verhandlungssichere Deutschkenntnisse in Wort und Schrift; Fließende Englischkenntnisse; Französischkenntnisse von Vorteil Was wir bieten Schlüsselrolle bei einem zentralen Akteur der Schweizer Energieinfrastruktur Hoher Gestaltungsspielraum mit direktem Einfluss auf Geschäftsbeziehung und Markterfolg Enge Zusammenarbeit mit erfahrenen Expertinnen und Experten sowie Entscheidungsträgern innerhalb von Hitachi Energy Attraktive Entwicklungs‑ und Karrieremöglichkeiten in einem global führenden Technologieunternehmen Beitrag zu einer sicheren, nachhaltigen und zukunftsfähigen Energieversorgung

jobs.title: Process Technician - Implantation 80 – 100% (f/m/d)
jobs.description: The opportunity Join a high performing team where your expertise truly makes an impact. As our Process Technician – Implantation you will be responsible for operating, monitoring, and maintaining advanced ion implantation processes used in semiconductor manufacturing processes This is your opportunity to shape technical decisions, contribute to long term tool strategies, and drive meaningful improvements in stability, uptime, and process performance. Are you someone who takes ownership, thrives in a fast-paced environment, and enjoys making a real impact? Then apply today! How you'll make an impact Production support for the designated area Analyze and make decision on how to release production lots (Lots on hold) Perform process top-up post machine aborts Operate and monitor ion implanters, ensuring parameters are set correctly and wafers are processed according to recipes Conduct in-process quality control checks, including visual inspections and data logging, to ensure product integrity Document and keep up-to-date all work instructions for the production personal as well as perform trainings and maintain all the training certification documentation up-to-date for the production personal Collaborate with engineering and manufacturing teams to optimize processes, improve yields, and resolve production issues Participate in continuous improvement initiatives, suggesting and implementing enhancements for production and processes Your background Associate's degree in science \/ production processes 2+ years of experience working as a process technician in a semiconductor manufacturing environment, specifically with ion implantation equipment Strong understanding of semiconductor device physics and fabrication processes Experience with various metrology tools used for in-process measurements Ability to read and interpret technical manuals Proficient in using computer-controlled equipment and data logging software German fluent, ability to communicate and read English Ability to work in a cleanroom environment and follow strict cleanroom protocols More about us Our flexible working practices help you optimize personal and business performance while creating an environment where all employees can develop their skills and grow.

jobs.title: Internship in Computational Chemistry 80 - 100% (f/m/d)
jobs.description: The opportunity The design of high voltage (HV) devices, such as gas circuit breakers (CB) and gas insulated switchgear (GIS), requires – among others –simulations and experiments for verification of insulation and switching performance. Additionally, the research activities in the area of gas circuit breakers are enhanced by various computer-aided-engineering tools. We are looking for an intern student with expertise in computational chemistry to contribute to the development of novel insulating materials for high-voltage power grid applications. The superior goal is the achievement of high-end quality of the product. The duration of the internship is 6 to 12 months. How you'll make an impact Contribute to technology development and research activities focusing on material analysis and characterization Support simulations of gas insulation systems using state of the art computational chemistry packages Study the relationship between microstructure and material characteristics and document findings and present test results to the team Your background B.Sc. degree in Material Science, Chemistry, Physics or similar discipline with focus on computer-aided methods Programming skills and experience in computational chemistry is an advantage Fluency in written and spoken English Team player and well organized Creative and able to work autonomously Laboratory and experimental experience is a plus More about us We are a technology‑driven company with a strong focus on innovation, continuous improvement, and sustainable manufacturing. We offer flexible working conditions, dedicated mentorship, and a supportive, inclusive environment where you can grow personally and professionally – empowering you to thrive while balancing life and work.

jobs.title: SAP Business Unit Lead Service
jobs.description: The opportunity Hitachi Energy is searching for an expert SAP Business Analyst who can bridge business needs and technology by driving end-to-end ERP solutions. This role involves analyzing requirements, defining scope, and translating complex business processes into SAP module specifications, while recommending improvements that enhance efficiency and performance. As a recognized subject matter expert, you'll shape operational plans, influence leadership, and lead the development of innovative processes and standards that deliver measurable impact. Strong communication skills and the ability to align stakeholders with strategic objectives are essential for success. How you'll make an impact Establish governance and organizational frameworks to ensure clear roles, responsibilities, and decision-making aligned with global strategies. Provide strategic oversight for global programs , ensuring adherence to standardized processes and full adoption of core systems. Gather and analyze business requirements , synthesizing input from stakeholders to align with business goals. Manage BU demand funnel toward SAP CoE in line with business archetype. Lead process harmonization and continuous improvement , eliminating template deviations and promoting best practices across regions. Monitor business process performance through key metrics, data analysis, and benchmarking against industry standards. Drive data governance and management , ensuring accuracy, consistency, and security across HUBs and systems. Assess and address training needs , delivering resources and sessions to enhance team competencies and performance. Champion change management initiatives , promoting global process adoption, minimizing local customizations, and securing stakeholder buy-in. Identify and mitigate risks related to system implementations and process changes, ensuring compliance with regulatory requirements. Lead and develop global teams , fostering accountability, collaboration, and professional growth through coaching and performance management. Collaborate on budget and demand planning , share best practices, and maintain regular communication with BU management. Your background Degree in Information Technology, Engineering, Business Administration, or related field. Minimum of 10 years of progressive experience in manufacturing, operations management, or related roles, with a proven track record of leading cross-functional teams and managing global projects. Strong leadership and change management skills, with the ability to influence and motivate global teams and stakeholders. In-depth knowledge of manufacturing, sales, logistics, service processes, systems, and process improvement methodologies (Lean, Six Sigma). Excellent communication and stakeholder engagement skills, capable of effectively conveying complex concepts and securing buy-in at all organizational levels. Analytical mindset with the ability to leverage data for performance monitoring, decision-making, and continuous improvement initiatives.

As a recognized subject matter expert, you'll shape operational plans, influence leadership, and lead the development of innovative processes and standards that deliver measurable impact. Strong communication skills and the ability to align stakeholders with strategic objectives are essential for success. How you'll make an impact Establish governance and organizational frameworks to ensure clear roles, responsibilities, and decision-making aligned with global strategies. Provide strategic oversight for global programs , ensuring adherence to standardized processes and full adoption of core systems. Gather and analyze business requirements , synthesizing input from stakeholders to align with business goals. Manage BU demand funnel toward SAP CoE in line with business archetype. Lead process harmonization and continuous improvement , eliminating template deviations and promoting best practices across regions. Monitor business process performance through key metrics, data analysis, and benchmarking against industry standards. Drive data governance and management , ensuring accuracy, consistency, and security across HUBs and systems. Assess and address training needs , delivering resources and sessions to enhance team competencies and performance. Champion change management initiatives , promoting global process adoption, minimizing local customizations, and securing stakeholder buy-in. Identify and mitigate risks related to system implementations and process changes, ensuring compliance with regulatory requirements. Lead and develop global teams , fostering accountability, collaboration, and professional growth through coaching and performance management. Collaborate on budget and demand planning , share best practices, and maintain regular communication with BU management. Your background Degree in Information Technology, Engineering, Business Administration, or related field. Minimum of 10 years of progressive experience in manufacturing, operations management, or related roles, with a proven track record of leading cross-functional teams and managing global projects. Strong leadership and change management skills, with the ability to influence and motivate global teams and stakeholders. In-depth knowledge of manufacturing, sales, logistics, service processes, systems, and process improvement methodologies (Lean, Six Sigma). Excellent communication and stakeholder engagement skills, capable of effectively conveying complex concepts and securing buy-in at all organizational levels. Analytical mindset with the ability to leverage data for performance monitoring, decision-making, and continuous improvement initiatives.

jobs.title: GPG Insulation & Components APQP Leader
jobs.description: The Opportunity As an GPG Insulation & Components APQP Leader you will be responsible for the implementation and maintenance of Foundation APQP essentials, according to the defined strategy and plan for GPG I&C Quality. You will collaborate with crossfunctional teams ensuring that Foundation APQP essentials are updated according to novelties and new risks. How you´ll make an impact Coordinate the development of Foundation APQP plan. Lead foundation APQP Plan related meetings, collecting and preparing documentation, being main connection point between QMT, Factories, Global experts and R&D. Participate in Foundation FMEA workshops and s upport HUBs and Factories to use foundation APQP essentials (to be cascaded to local products). Support R&D organization to use foundation APQP essentials for new product developments. Support ToT projects to ensure proper transfer of APQP essentials. Act as GPG I&C Plato Admin, train crossfunctional teams, support the organization on the usage and ensure that foundation content is updated . Proactive identification of technology novelties to drive updates in foundation APQP essentials and c ollaborate with GPG Operations to identify global process changes that might affect to foundation APQP essentials ( e.g. from best practices to requirement). Your b ackground Technical degree from the University . 3-5 years of experience with APQP ( preferably in industries like Automotive or Wind). Strong knowledge of APQP processes, FMEA, PPAP, SPC, and MSA methodologies. Experience with Plato is an advatage . Excellent analytical and problem-solving skills with the ability to identify and implement process improvements. Proven ability to lead cross-functional teams and facilitate effective collaboration among diverse stakeholders. Fluency in English What w e o ffer Collective agreement and flexible working hours. Health care and wellness allowance. Career opportunities within Hitachi Energ y, locally and globally. Mentor support during onboarding. Training and development programs. A diverse workplace with 70+ nationalities in Sweden. Supplementary parental leave compensation. Access to an Employee Benefit Portal with discounts. More a bout u s At Hitachi Energy, you'll work in an exciting, collaborative environment delivering world-class technology. We value personal development and diversity, and we are committed to building a sustainable energy future. We are growing and welcome applicants who may not meet every requirement—your potential matters. Applications are reviewed on an ongoing basis, so don't wait—apply today! For questions about the position, contact Recruiting Manager Yolanda Villaseca at yolanda.villaseca@hitachienergy.com . Union representatives: Sveriges Ingenjörer : Håkan Blomquist +46 107-38 31 52 , Ledarna : Frank Hollstedt +46 107 38 70 42 . All other questions can be directed to Talent Partner Michael Blomberg , michael.blomberg@hitachienergy.com

jobs.title: High Voltage Testing Engineer 80 – 100% (f/m/d)
jobs.description: The opportunity Our local product group Insulation & Components is a global technology leader in the design and manufacture of high-voltage transformer bushings. Renowned international companies across the electrical industry—including power generation, transmission, and distribution—trust our solutions. We hold a leading worldwide position in high-voltage bushing technology. To strengthen our operations, we are looking for a High Voltage Testing Engineer to join our high-voltage testing team at our MICAFIL bushing factory. If you are passionate about high-voltage testing and want to actively contribute to the future of our production processes, this is your opportunity to make a real impact. How you'll make an impact Plan and execute electrical and thermal type tests as well as special tests on high-voltage bushings (in-house and externally), considering available resources and budget Assist and lead Factory Acceptance Tests (FATs), including in-house FATs with customers Ensure the full functionality and availability of three high-voltage laboratories in collaboration with the team Lead and manage laboratory-related projects Ensure safety of equipment and personnel through training, instructions, and compliance with safety standards Secure quality, traceability, and compliance of bushing process steps through process instructions Perform routine, type, and special tests as required Continuously challenge and improve procedures and instructions as part of Continuous Improvement Processes (CIP) As part of the Operations department, actively support and contribute to daily business activities Your background Bachelor's or Master's degree in Electrical Engineering or equivalent Professional experience in high-voltage technology, testing, or electrical machines, ideally in a production or industrial environment Solid understanding of ISO 9001 and ISO 17025 Business-fluent German and good English, both written and spoken Strong team spirit, high social competence, and enjoyment of working in a multicultural environment Flexible mindset with a strong enthusiasm for technology Proactive and solution-oriented approach to problem-solving More about us Our flexible working practices help you optimize personal and business performance while creating an environment where all employees can develop their skills and grow.

jobs.title: Global Data, Analytics and Artificial Intelligence - BU Transformers
jobs.description: The opportunity Hitachi Energy is seeking a Global Data, Analytics and Artificial Intelligence Manager for the Business Unit Transformers to join our team shaping the future of energy through digital innovation, data‑driven insights, and AI‑enabled solutions. In this role, you will drive data‑ and AI‑enabled value creation by converting business strategy into prioritized AI initiatives, ensuring measurable impact across operations, decision‑making, and innovation. This position offers the opportunity to grow your career within the newly established Digital Transformation Organization, collaborate with global stakeholders, and shape the future of Transformer solutions. How you will make an impact Shape the future of data, analytics, and AI by setting a clear strategic direction that drives meaningful business outcomes at scale. Turn real business challenges into AI opportunities that improve decisions, performance, and competitive advantage. Build and lead a high‑impact AI portfolio, taking ideas from early concepts to scaled solutions that create measurable value. Champion responsible and trusted AI by embedding strong governance, ethics, and data standards into everything delivered. Elevate data as a strategic asset by improving quality, consistency, and access to enable powerful insights and AI solutions. Lead diverse, cross‑functional teams to deliver AI initiatives that are practical, scalable, and business‑ready. Learn from results to continuously refine approaches, scale successes, and amplify business impact. Build strong partnerships with business and digital leaders to align priorities and accelerate AI adoption. Strengthen organizational capabilities through targeted learning, clear messaging, and a strong data‑driven mindset. Drive change with confidence by guiding stakeholders through transformation and ensuring responsible adoption of new solutions. Inspire, develop, and grow high‑performing Data, Analytics, and AI teams aligned behind a shared vision. Your background Master's degree in computer science, Artificial Intelligence, Data Science, or a related field. Relevant certifications (e.g., CAIP, advanced AI\/ML certifications) highly valued. Minimum 15+ years of experience in roles related to business process management or program implementation in the manufacturing industry. Effective communication skills with a proven ability to engage diverse stakeholders at all levels, fostering a collaborative environment to drive initiatives. Demonstrated competence in influencing and motivating teams to adopt new processes, reduce resistance, and achieve successful transitions to globally harmonized practices. Deep understanding of data, analytics, AI\/GenAI technologies, machine learning and analytics platforms. Strong analytical skills to tackle complex data and\/or AI challenges. Awareness of ethical considerations in AI development and deployment. Ability to bridge strategy, governance, and execution.

Minimum 15+ years of experience in roles related to business process management or program implementation in the manufacturing industry. Effective communication skills with a proven ability to engage diverse stakeholders at all levels, fostering a collaborative environment to drive initiatives. Demonstrated competence in influencing and motivating teams to adopt new processes, reduce resistance, and achieve successful transitions to globally harmonized practices. Deep understanding of data, analytics, AI\/GenAI technologies, machine learning and analytics platforms. Strong analytical skills to tackle complex data and\/or AI challenges. Awareness of ethical considerations in AI development and deployment. Ability to bridge strategy, governance, and execution.
descriptionByLocale.en: The opportunity Hitachi Energy is seeking a Global Data, Analytics and Artificial Intelligence Manager for the Business Unit Transformers to join our team shaping the future of energy through digital innovation, data‑driven insights, and AI‑enabled solutions. In this role, you will drive data‑ and AI‑enabled value creation by converting business strategy into prioritized AI initiatives, ensuring measurable impact across operations, decision‑making, and innovation. This position offers the opportunity to grow your career within the newly established Digital Transformation Organization, collaborate with global stakeholders, and shape the future of Transformer solutions. How you will make an impact Shape the future of data, analytics, and AI by setting a clear strategic direction that drives meaningful business outcomes at scale. Turn real business challenges into AI opportunities that improve decisions, performance, and competitive advantage. Build and lead a high‑impact AI portfolio, taking ideas from early concepts to scaled solutions that create measurable value. Champion responsible and trusted AI by embedding strong governance, ethics, and data standards into everything delivered. Elevate data as a strategic asset by improving quality, consistency, and access to enable powerful insights and AI solutions. Lead diverse, cross‑functional teams to deliver AI initiatives that are practical, scalable, and business‑ready. Learn from results to continuously refine approaches, scale successes, and amplify business impact. Build strong partnerships with business and digital leaders to align priorities and accelerate AI adoption. Strengthen organizational capabilities through targeted learning, clear messaging, and a strong data‑driven mindset. Drive change with confidence by guiding stakeholders through transformation and ensuring responsible adoption of new solutions. Inspire, develop, and grow high‑performing Data, Analytics, and AI teams aligned behind a shared vision. Your background Master's degree in computer science, Artificial Intelligence, Data Science, or a related field. Relevant certifications (e.g., CAIP, advanced AI\/ML certifications) highly valued. Minimum 15+ years of experience in roles related to business process management or program implementation in the manufacturing industry. Effective communication skills with a proven ability to engage diverse stakeholders at all levels, fostering a collaborative environment to drive initiatives. Demonstrated competence in influencing and motivating teams to adopt new processes, reduce resistance, and achieve successful transitions to globally harmonized practices. Deep understanding of data, analytics, AI\/GenAI technologies, machine learning and analytics platforms. Strong analytical skills to tackle complex data and\/or AI challenges. jobs.title: Engineering Global Sustainability Leader
jobs.location: Remote - North Carolina, United States of America
jobs.description: The opportunity Sustainability starts at the design table—and this role puts you right at the center of that impact. As the Engineering Design Sustainability Lead , you will shape how sustainability is embedded into global engineering decisions across our transformer business. This is a highly visible, strategic role where engineering excellence meets environmental responsibility. You will lead the shift from downstream sustainability activities to design‑led outcomes, ensuring product‑level carbon reduction, material optimization, and environmental performance are built into every design choice from the outset. You will work across global engineering teams to translate sustainability ambitions into measurable, auditable product outcomes—balancing cost, safety, quality, and performance. With increasing expectations from customers, regulators, and stakeholders for credible product‑level data, your work will directly support compliance, ESG reporting, and long‑term business resilience. If you thrive at the intersection of engineering, data, and sustainability—and enjoy influencing without direct authority—this role offers a unique opportunity to shape the future of sustainable product design at scale. How you'll make an impact Define and drive a global, design‑led sustainability strategy within Engineering. Embed sustainability into engineering standards, processes, and decision frameworks. Reduce product‑level carbon footprint through smart design, material, and architecture choices. Establish sustainability targets, KPIs, and traceable product‑level data at BOM level. Partner across engineering, R&D, supply chain, digital and operations teams globally. Strengthen governance, reporting, and audit‑ready sustainability metrics. Build capability and foster ownership of sustainable design within engineering teams. Translate regulatory and customer sustainability expectations into engineering requirements. Responsible to ensure compliance with applicable external and internal regulations, procedures, and guidelines. Your background Master's degree or PhD in Engineering or a closely related sustainability discipline. 10+ years of engineering experience, including 5+ years in sustainability‑focused roles. Proven success delivering design‑led sustainability or product carbon reduction initiatives. Strong knowledge of lifecycle assessment, sustainable materials, and carbon accounting. Experience leading global, cross‑functional initiatives and influencing senior stakeholders. Strong analytical mindset with a data‑driven approach to decision‑making. More about us At Hitachi Energy , sustainability, innovation, and safety are core to how we design the future of energy. You'll work in a global, diverse environment where ideas are valued and impact is visible. We offer meaningful opportunities to grow, lead, and shape industry‑leading solutions with purpose.

jobs.title: Prozess- und Anlagentechniker/in 80 - 100% (w/m/d)
jobs.description: Über die Position Für unsere Industrie 4.0-fähige Produktion von Leistungshalbleitern suchen wir eine engagierte und technisch versierte Persönlichkeit als Process & Equipment Technician. Mit deinem Know-how in Systemtechnik und Automatisierung sorgst du für eine hohe Anlagenverfügbarkeit, behebst Störungen, treibst Verbesserungen voran und unterstützt deine Kolleg\/innen bei technischen Fragestellungen. Gemeinsam mit dem Engineering-Team bringst du unsere Fertigung auf das nächste Level und begleitest die Integration neuer Anlagen in laufende Produktionsprozesse. Das kannst du bewirken Kalibrierung, Wartung und Instandhaltung von Anlagen und Komponenten Eigenständige Fehlersuche und -behebung an komplexen Produktionssystemen (2.\/3. Level) Präventive und zustandsabhängige Instandhaltung, um Stillstände zu minimieren Dokumentation und Kommunikation des Anlagenzustands und technischer Anpassungen Überwachung der Prozesskonformität und Qualitätskontrolle (SPC) Umsetzung von Verbesserungsmassnahmen in Zusammenarbeit mit Engineering und Support Schulung und Unterstützung von Kolleg\/innen bei technischen Themen Sicherstellung der Arbeitssicherheit in allen Tätigkeiten Das bringst du mit Abgeschlossene Ausbildung EFZ als Automatiker\/in, Elektroniker\/in oder Polymechaniker\/in Fundierte elektrotechnische Grundausbildung, idealerweise mit Weiterbildung als Systemtechniker\/in Mehrjährige Erfahrung in der Instandhaltung oder Automatisierung von Produktionsanlagen Sehr gute Kenntnisse in Steuerungstechnik, Pneumatik und\/oder Roboterprogrammierung Strukturierte, analytische und selbstständige Arbeitsweise Gute Deutschkenntnisse (C1) und Englischkenntnisse (B2) Mehr über uns Sicherer Arbeitsplatz in einem zukunftsorientierten Unternehmen Ein kollegiales Team mit kurzen Entscheidungswegen Attraktive Anstellungsbedingungen und Entwicklungsmöglichkeiten

jobs.title: Talent Acquisition Partner 1 year contract (f/m/d)
jobs.description: The opportunity We're looking for a Talent Acquisition Partner to join our dynamic team and support our German-speaking market. In this role, you'll work closely local stakeholders and colleagues to attract top talent, drive local initiatives, and elevate candidate experience. If you're passionate about recruitment and thrive in a fast-paced, collaborative environment, we'd love to hear from you! How you'll make an impact Drive end-to-end recruitment for roles on the German-speaking market across multiple functions and Business Units Act as an advisor to hiring managers, providing market insights and talent solutions Partner with local HR and business stakeholders to work actively on their hiring needs and build proactive pipelines Champion diversity, equity, and inclusion in all hiring practices Represent the company at external events and build strong employer branding initiatives Your background At least 3-5 years of experience in talent acquisition in a similar role ideally combined with experience working in an international or matrixed environment Strong stakeholder management and influencing skills across all levels of the organization Good understanding of German-speaking labor market is mandatory Deep understanding of sourcing strategies, interview techniques, and assessment tools Fluency in English and German is mandatory (C1 level or mother tongue) Passionate about people, data-driven decision-making, and delivering results

jobs.title: R&D Engineer - High Voltage Circuit Breakers 80 - 100% (f/m/d)
jobs.description: The opportunity For our Business Unit High Voltage Products in Zurich-Oerlikon, we are looking for an R&D Engineer to strengthen our team for High Voltage Circuit Breakers development. If you are passionate about solving highly complex multi-physical problems and want to work with world-leading experts to develop best-in-class High Voltage Circuit Breakers, this is your chance. Join our innovative team and be part of an international working environment in Zurich. How you'll make an impact Execute product-development-related engineering activities for specific R&D projects from concept to series implementation, ensuring technical alignment with cross-functional experts and project stakeholders Support the Design Lead in product development topics and ensure structured documentation of development activities Plan and supervise assembly, disassembly and inspection of prototypes together with other experts Contribute to the continuous improvement of R&D processes and activities Your background Master's degree in mechanical engineering or a closely related subject from a Polytechnical\/Technical University Working experience in an R&D environment is a plus Experience in project management is a plus Good know-how in 3D CAD and FEM software tools required High expectations on quality of work in all regards Independent, proactive and goal-oriented personality Practically oriented and hands-on way of working Self-motivated, committed, willing to learn Excellent communication skills Excellent command of English and German, both written and spoken More about us At Hitachi Energy, our culture is built on collaboration, diversity and continuous learning. We believe that the best ideas come from working together across cultures and disciplines, embracing different perspectives and fostering an inclusive environment. We encourage curiosity and innovation, providing opportunities for personal and professional growth. Join us and be part of a global team that is shaping a sustainable energy future.

jobs.title: Factory Digitalization and Automation Engineer 80 - 100% (f/m/d)
jobs.description: The opportunity Join our team in Molinazzo di Monteggio, Switzerland as a Digitalization & Automation Engineer and support our factory's digital transformation by designing, developing, and implementing automation, digitalization, and data-driven improvement solutions. How you'll make an impact Analyze manufacturing and business processes to identify automation and digitalization opportunities Design and implement solutions for process automation (industrial or office), digital workflows and paperless processes, as well as production data collection and analysis Collaborate with Operations, IT, Quality, and Continuous Improvement teams to define functional and technical requirements Develop and maintain KPI dashboards (OEE, productivity, quality, downtime) and data acquisition systems (PLC, sensors, MES) Manage technology suppliers and system integrators Ensure compliance with safety, quality, and cybersecurity standards Train end users and support change management activities Your background Master's or bachelor's degree in engineering (Automation, Mechatronics, Computer Science, Electronics, or similar) Solid understanding of control logic and process automation, operational process digitalization Experience with MES, ERP, or traceability systems Knowledge of Python, SQL, Power BI \/ Tableau, RPA tools (for example: Power Automate) and industrial IoT solutions Experience with data analysis and reporting tools Experience in manufacturing or industrial environments Technical English (written and spoken) and Italian at least intermediate Familiarity of Lean Six Sigma methodologies and industrial automation systems (PLC, HMI, SCADA, IBA) is an advantage More about us Our flexible working practices help you optimize personal and business performance while creating an environment where all employees can develop their skills and grow.

descriptionByLocale.it: ## Descrizione Unisciti al nostro team di Molinazzo di Monteggio, Svizzera come ingegnere di digitalizzazione e automazione e supporta la trasformazione digitale della nostra fabbrica progettando, sviluppando e implementando soluzioni di automazione, digitalizzazione e miglioramento basate sui dati. ## Mansioni - Analizzare processi produttivi e aziendali per identificare opportunità di automazione e digitalizzazione - Progettazione e realizzazione di soluzioni per l'automazione dei processi (industriale o ufficio), flussi di lavoro digitali e processi senza carta, nonché raccolta e analisi dei dati di produzione - Collaborare con i team Operazioni, IT, Qualità e Miglioramento continuo per definire requisiti funzionali e tecnici - Sviluppare e mantenere cruscotti KPI (OEE, produttività, qualità, downtime) e sistemi di acquisizione dati (PLC, sensori, MES) - Gestione di fornitori di tecnologia e integratori di sistema - Garantire il rispetto degli standard di sicurezza, qualità e sicurezza informatica - Utenti finali e attività di gestione dei cambiamenti di supporto # Requisiti # - Master o laurea in ingegneria (Automazione, Meccatronica, Informatica, Elettronica, o simili) - Solida comprensione della logica di controllo e dell'automazione dei processi, digitalizzazione dei processi operativi - Esperienza con sistemi MES, ERP o tracciabilità - Conoscenza di Python, SQL, Power BI / Tableau, strumenti RPA (ad esempio: Power Automate) e soluzioni IoT industriali - Esperienza con l'analisi dei dati e strumenti di reportistica - Esperienza in ambienti industriali o industriali - Inglese tecnico (scritto e parlato) e italiano almeno intermedio - La familiarità delle metodologie Lean Six Sigma e dei sistemi di automazione industriale (PLC, HMI, SCADA, IBA) è un vantaggio # Cosa # Le nostre pratiche di lavoro flessibili ti aiutano a ottimizzare le prestazioni personali e aziendali creando un ambiente in cui tutti i dipendenti possono sviluppare le loro competenze e crescere.
descriptionByLocale.en: ## Descrizione The opportunity Join our team in Molinazzo di Monteggio, Switzerland as a Digitalization & Automation Engineer and support our factory's digital transformation by designing, developing, and implementing automation, digitalization, and data-driven improvement solutions. ## Mansioni - Analyze manufacturing and business processes to identify automation and digitalization opportunities - Design and implement solutions for process automation (industrial or office), digital workflows and paperless processes, as well as production data collection and analysis - Collaborate with Operations, IT, Quality, and Continuous Improvement teams to define functional and technical requirements - Develop and maintain KPI dashboards (OEE, productivity, quality, downtime) and data acquisition systems (PLC, sensors, MES) - Manage technology suppliers and system integrators - Ensure compliance with safety, quality, and cybersecurity standards - Train end users and support change management activities ## Requisiti - Master's or bachelor's degree in engineering (Automation, Mechatronics, Computer Science, Electronics, or similar) - Solid understanding of control logic and process automation, operational process digitalization - Experience with MES, ERP, or traceability systems - Knowledge of Python, SQL, Power BI / Tableau, RPA tools (for example: Power Automate) and industrial IoT solutions - Experience with data analysis and reporting tools - Experience in manufacturing or industrial environments - Technical English (written and spoken) and Italian at least intermediate - Familiarity of Lean Six Sigma methodologies and industrial automation systems (PLC, HMI, SCADA, IBA) is an advantage ## Cosa offriamo - More about us Our flexible working practices help you optimize personal and business performance while creating an environment where all employees can develop their skills and grow.
descriptionByLocale.de: Descrizione Die Chance: Treten Sie unserem Team in Molinazzo di Monteggio, Schweiz, als Digitalisierungs- und Automatisierungsingenieur bei und unterstützen Sie die digitale Transformation unserer Fabrik, indem Sie Automatisierungs-, Digitalisierungs- und datengetriebene Verbesserungslösungen entwerfen, entwickeln und implementieren. ## Mansioni – Analyse von Fertigungs- und Geschäftsprozessen, um Automatisierungs- und Digitalisierungsmöglichkeiten zu identifizieren – Lösungen für Prozessautomatisierung (Industrie- oder Büroentwicklung), digitale Arbeitsabläufe und papierlose Prozesse sowie für die Sammlung und Analyse von Produktionsdaten zu entwerfen und umzusetzen – Zusammenarbeit mit Teams für Betrieb, IT, Qualität und kontinuierliche Verbesserung, um funktionale und technische Anforderungen zu definieren – Entwicklung und Pflege von KPI-Dashboards (OEE, Produktivität, Qualität, Ausfallzeit) und Datenerfassungssysteme (SPS, Sensoren, MES) - Verwaltung von Technologiezulieferern und Systemintegratoren - Sicherstellung der Einhaltung von Sicherheits-, Qualitäts- und Cybersicherheitsstandards - Ausbildung von Endnutzern und Unterstützung von Change-Management-Aktivitäten ## Requisiti - Master- oder Bachelorabschluss in Ingenieurwesen (Automatisierung, Mechatronik, Informatik, Elektronik oder Ähnliches) - Solides Verständnis von Regelungslogik und Prozessautomatisierung, operativer Prozessdigitalisierung - Erfahrung mit MES-, ERP- oder Rückverfolgungssystemen – Kenntnisse in Python, SQL, Power BI / Tableau, RPA-Tools (z. B. Power Automate) und industriellen IoT-Lösungen – Erfahrung mit Datenanalyse- und Berichtswerkzeugen – Erfahrung in Fertigungs- oder Industrieumgebungen – Technisches Englisch (geschrieben und gesprochen) und Italienisch mindestens auf Mittelstufe – Vertrautheit mit Lean Six Sigma-Methoden und industriellen Automatisierungssystemen (SPS, HMI, SCADA, IBA) ist ein Vorteil ## Cosa offriamo – Mehr über uns Unsere flexiblen Arbeitspraktiken helfen Ihnen, persönliche und geschäftliche Leistung zu optimieren und gleichzeitig ein Umfeld zu schaffen, in dem alle Mitarbeiter ihre Fähigkeiten entwickeln und wachsen können.
descriptionByLocale.fr: Descrizione L'opportunité Rejoignez notre équipe à Molinazzo di Monteggio, Suisse en tant qu'ingénieur Digitalisation & Automation et soutenez la transformation numérique de notre usine en concevant, développant et implémentant des solutions d'automatisation, de numérisation et d'amélioration des données. Les hommes - Analyser les processus de fabrication et d'affaires pour identifier les possibilités d'automatisation et de numérisation - Concevoir et mettre en œuvre des solutions pour l'automatisation des processus (industriels ou de bureau), les flux de travail numériques et les processus sans papier, ainsi que la collecte et l'analyse des données de production - Collaborer avec les équipes Opérations, TI, Qualité et Amélioration continue pour définir les exigences fonctionnelles et techniques - Développer et entretenir des tableaux de bord KPI (EEE, productivité, qualité, temps d'arrêt) et des systèmes d'acquisition de données (PLC, capteurs, MES) - Gérer les fournisseurs de technologie et les intégrateurs de systèmes - Assurer le respect des normes de sécurité, de qualité et de cybersécurité - Former les utilisateurs finaux et soutenir les activités de gestion du changement ## Requisiti - Maîtrise ou baccalauréat en ingénierie (Automation, Mécatronique, Informatique, Électronique ou similaire) - Compréhension solide de la logique de contrôle et de l'automatisation des processus, numérisation des processus opérationnels - Expérience des systèmes MES, ERP ou de traçabilité - Connaissance de Python, SQL, Power BI / Tableau, outils RPA (par exemple: Power Automate) et solutions IoT industrielles - Expérience des outils d ' analyse et de communication des données - Expérience dans les environnements industriels ou manufacturiers - Anglais technique (écrit et parlé) et italien au moins intermédiaire - Connaissance des méthodologies Lean Six Sigma et des systèmes d'automatisation industrielle (PLC, HMI, SCADA, IBA) est un avantage Cosa offriamo - Plus sur nous Nos pratiques de travail flexibles vous aident à optimiser la performance personnelle et commerciale tout en créant un environnement où tous les employés peuvent développer leurs compétences et grandir.

jobs.title: Troubleshooting Improvement Specialist 40 - 50% (f/m/d)
jobs.description: Über die Position Der Bereich GIS (gasisolierte Schaltanlagen) steht für höchste Qualität, Sicherheit und Effizienz – und genau hier setzt deine Rolle an. Als Troubleshooting Improvement Specialist unterstützt du aktiv bei der Analyse und nachhaltigen Verbesserung von Qualitäts- und Produktionsprozessen. Du arbeitest eng mit unterschiedlichen internen Fachbereichen zusammen, bringst neue Perspektiven ein und hilfst dabei, wiederkehrende Probleme systematisch zu identifizieren und zu lösen. Das kannst du bewirken Unterstützung bei der Analyse von Qualitätsmeldungen und Produktionsproblemen Mitarbeit an der kontinuierlichen Verbesserung von Troubleshooting-Prozessen Enge Zusammenarbeit mit internen Schnittstellen wie Produktion, Qualität, Engineering und Planung Unterstützung bei der Auswertung von Produktions- und Qualitätsdaten Dokumentation von Maßnahmen und Nachverfolgung von Verbesserungsinitiativen Mitarbeit bei internen Reviews und Audits Das bringst du mit Laufendes Studium oder abgeschlossene Ausbildung im Bereich Elektrotechnik, Maschinenbau, Industrial Engineering, Wirtschaftsingenieurwesen oder vergleichbar Erste praktische Erfahrung im industriellen Umfeld von Vorteil, idealerweise mit Bezug zu Produktion, Industrialisierung, Digitalisierung oder Prozessautomatisierung Starkes Interesse an Produktionsprozessen, Qualität und Problemanalyse sowie eine analytische, strukturierte und eigenständige Arbeitsweise Gute Deutsch- und Englischkenntnisse Grundkenntnisse in SAP, DSP, MES oder Datenanalyse-Tools sind ein Plus Mehr über uns Bei Hitachi Energy glauben wir an die Kraft der Vielfalt, Zusammenarbeit und Innovation. Unsere Kultur ist geprägt von kontinuierlichem Lernen und dem gemeinsamen Ziel, nachhaltige Energielösungen zu gestalten. Wir fördern ein inklusives Arbeitsumfeld, in dem jede Meinung zählt und du deine Ideen einbringen kannst – unabhängig von Herkunft, Geschlecht oder Hintergrund.

jobs.title: Operator/in Varistorenfertigung 80 - 100% (w/m/d)
jobs.description: Über die Position Als Operator\/in Varistorenfertigung bei Hitachi Energy am Standort Wettingen bist du ein zentraler Bestandteil unserer Produktion. Du verantwortest das Einrichten und Bedienen von Fertigungsanlagen zur Herstellung von Metalloxidvaristoren und stellst sicher, dass unsere Produkte höchsten Qualitäts-, Sicherheits- und Prozessstandards entsprechen. Dank deiner Erfahrung behältst du auch bei Abweichungen den Überblick, analysierst Ursachen und unterstützt aktiv die Stabilität und kontinuierliche Verbesserung unserer Produktionsprozesse. Das kannst du bewirken Einrichten, Bedienen und Überwachen verschiedener Produktionsanlagen zur Herstellung von Metalloxidvaristoren Sorgfältige und qualitätsbewusste Ausführung aller relevanten Prozess- und Arbeitsschritte Überwachung der Produktionsprozesse sowie frühzeitiges Erkennen und Analysieren von Abweichungen Durchführung von Qualitätskontrollen und Sicherstellung der Produktkonformität gemäss Vorgaben Dokumentation von Produktions- und Prozesswerten in den vorgesehenen Systemen Sicherstellung der Einhaltung von Sicherheits-, Qualitäts- und Umweltvorschriften (HSE) Aktive Unterstützung bei der Optimierung von Abläufen und kontinuierlichen Verbesserungen Gewährleistung von Ordnung und Sauberkeit im Arbeitsbereich gemäss 5S-Prinzipien Das bringst du mit Abgeschlossene technische oder handwerkliche Berufsausbildung (z. B. Produktionsmechaniker, Automatiker, Anlagenführer oder vergleichbar) Mehrjährige Erfahrung im industriellen Produktionsumfeld oder in der Anlagenbedienung Gutes technisches Verständnis sowie sicherer Umgang mit Produktions- und Prozessanlagen Strukturierte, zuverlässige und qualitätsorientierte Arbeitsweise Teamfähigkeit, Verantwortungsbewusstsein und selbstständige Arbeitsweise Gute Deutschkenntnisse in Wort und Schrift Mehr über uns Hitachi Energy steht für innovative Technologien und nachhaltige Energielösungen weltweit. Bei uns erwartet dich ein sicherer Arbeitsplatz in einem zukunftsorientierten Unternehmen, spannende Entwicklungsmöglichkeiten, ein kollegiales und wertschätzendes Arbeitsumfeld sowie attraktive Sozialleistungen.

jobs.title: Product Manager Network Control – Power Applications (f/m/d)
jobs.description: The opportunity At Hitachi Energy, we're shaping the future of sustainable energy systems—and you can be at the heart of it. As Product Manager for Power Applications (EMS \/ ADMS \/ DERMS), you will own the vision, strategy, and evolution of the advanced power and network analysis applications that power our Network Control (NC) portfolio. This is a highly visible, global role where technical depth meets strategic influence, and where your ideas can directly transform how power systems are analysed and optimised in real time. You'll work across a sophisticated ecosystem of products including EMS, ADMS, DERMS and more ensuring that our power applications are aligned, scalable, and future-ready. A key focus of this role is identifying game‑changing opportunities to apply AI in power system analysis , redefining what's possible for grid operations worldwide. How you'll make an impact Define and drive the product vision, roadmap, and long-term strategy for power applications within the NC portfolio Lead the end-to-end product lifecycle, from requirements and design through testing, release, and post-launch support Shape and evolve advanced applications such as State Estimation, Power Flow (balanced & unbalanced), Security Analysis, Transient Stability, Dynamic Assessment, and Optimal Power Flow Collaborate with global R&D, Sales, Pre-sales, Marketing, and Customer Service teams to prioritize features and ensure successful delivery Identify and champion AI-driven innovations that revolutionize real-time power system analysis and optimization Monitor market trends and competitors to uncover differentiation and growth opportunities Define and track KPIs to measure product performance and customer value Support pricing strategies in partnership with Finance and assist Sales with technical expertise during pre- and post-sales engagements Engage directly with customers and partners, representing Hitachi Energy at industry conferences and events Your background Strong experience in product management, preferably within power systems, utilities, or complex software portfolios Deep understanding of power system analysis applications such as power flow, stability analysis, security analysis, and optimization Proven ability to work cross-functionally in a global, matrixed environment Strategic mindset combined with hands-on execution skills across the full product lifecycle Passion for innovation, with a strong interest in AI and advanced analytics applied to energy systems Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills, including customer-facing experience Ability to translate complex technical concepts into clear product strategies and business value More about us Join us and become part of a diverse, collaborative, and purpose-driven organization where your work has real-world impact, and your ideas help shape the energy systems of tomorrow.
