Mehrspindelportalfräsmaschinen, Zylinderschleifmaschinen, Mehrblattsägen, Fräs- sowie Kehlmaschinen) Prüfanlagen & Zuschnitt: Eigenständiges Einrichten und Bedienen elektrischer Prüfanlagen sowie verschiedener Besäumanlagen Steuerung von Aufträgen: Du koordinierst und steuerst Fertigungsaufträge eigenverantwortlich und stellst eine termingerechte Abarbeitung sicher Qualitätsprüfung: Du führst regelmäßige Qualitätsprüfungen an bearbeiteten Bauteilen durch – von Einzelteilen bis hin zu Kleinserien Wartung und Instandhaltung: Du übernimmst die Wartung und den Unterhalt der eingesetzten Maschinen und sorgst für deren zuverlässige Einsatzbereitschaft Überwachung von Hilfsmitteln: Du behältst den Einsatz von Fertigungshilfsmitteln im Blick und stellst deren Verfügbarkeit und Funktionalität sicher Transport & Logistik: Du bedienst verschiedene Flurförderfahrzeuge und unterstützt so einen reibungslosen Materialfluss in der Produktion Das bringst Du mit Abgeschlossene Ausbildung als Polymechaniker, Industriemechaniker, Formenbauer, Schreiner oder in einem ähnlichen Beruf mit mehrjähriger Erfahrung in einer vergleichbaren Position Gute Deutschkenntnisse in Wort und Schrift, Englischkenntnisse von Vorteil Du bist teamfähig, arbeitest selbstständig und bringst ein hohes Maß an Verantwortungsbewusstsein mit Du besitzt ein ausgezeichnetes Verständnis für technische Zeichnungen und Stücklisten Du bist flexibel, zeigst Eigeninitiative und bringst eine hohe Leistungsbereitschaft mit Unternehmerisches Denken und eine ausgeprägte Kundenorientierung zeichnen dich aus Mehr über uns Unsere flexiblen Arbeitsmethoden helfen Dir, Deine beruflichen Leistungen zu optimieren, und schaffen ein Umfeld, in dem alle Mitarbeiter ihre Fähigkeiten entwickeln und wachsen können.
descriptionByLocale.de: Die Möglichkeit Werde Teil eines engagierten Teams und übernimm Verantwortung beim Einrichten und Bedienen eines vielseitigen und anspruchsvollen Maschinenparks. Du sorgst für reibungslose Abläufe in der Fertigung, steuerst Aufträge eigenständig, führst Qualitätsprüfungen durch und stellst die Einsatzbereitschaft der Maschinen sicher. Mit technischem Know-how, Eigeninitiative und Teamgeist trägst Du aktiv zur Effizienz und Weiterentwicklung unserer Produktionsprozesse bei. Unsere Fabrik in Klingnau steht unseren Kunden als Kompetenzzentrum für die hochpräzise Kunststoffverarbeitung auf Weltniveau zur Verfügung. Der Standort lebt von einem flexiblen und motivierten Team mit einer flachen Hierarchie und kurzen Entscheidungswegen. Durch den stetigen Ausbau unserer Kompetenzen im Bereich der Kunststoffverarbeitung als auch durch die ständige Verbesserung unserer Geschäftsprozesse offerieren wir unseren Kunden innovative, zuverlässige, individuelle und sichere Lösungen für Ihre Anliegen in den Segmenten Stromerzeugung\/-verteilung, Railway, Raumfahrt, Service und Industrie. Diesen stellen wir Ihnen zu angemessenen Preisen, termingerecht in der gewünschten Qualität zur Verfügung. Wir sichern unsere Wettbewerbsfähigkeit durch Automatisierung und Optimierung unserer Fertigungslinien, um damit noch schneller und flexibler auf weltweite Markt und Kundenanforderungen eingehen zu können. jobs.title: Field Service Technician 80 - 100% (f/m/d)
jobs.description: The Opportunity As a Field Service Technician, you will play a key role in maintaining the reliable operation of traction transformers across customer sites and in our factory. You will perform hands-on repairs, diagnose technical issues, and support customers directly. With your practical expertise, you will collaborate closely with internal teams and contribute to continuous product and process improvements. How you'll make an impact Always follow safety standards and company procedures during both field (60%) and factory (40%) activities. Perform on-site repairs and servicing of traction transformers, delivering prompt and professional solutions. Conduct field repairs, electrical diagnostic testing, and oil treatment at customer locations. Support failure investigations through disassembly, root-cause analysis, and the preparation of technical reports. Provide customers with technical support and training on equipment operation and basic maintenance. Contribute to small improvement projects aimed at boosting quality, efficiency, and service processes. Prepare service documentation, including work reports, material usage, quality inspection cards, and repair records. Collaborate with engineering, quality, project management, and sales teams to resolve escalated technical issues. In the factory, ensure effective coordination and execution of repair work alongside the Service team. Your background Completed technical apprenticeship or degree in Electrical\/Electrotechnical\/Maintenance fields. Minimum of 1–2 years of field service or technical repair experience, ideally in an industrial environment. Ability to read and interpret technical drawings, electrical cabling diagrams, and schematics. Strong analytical and troubleshooting skills for solving complex technical problems. Customer-oriented communicator, fluent in English; French is an advantage. Independent, self-driven, and flexible with working hours. Valid driving licence. More about us Our flexible working practices help you optimize personal and business performance while creating an environment where all employees can develop their skills and grow

jobs.title: Electrical Engineer – Standards Compliance Expert for Dry Transformers 80 – 100% (f/m/d)
jobs.description: The opportunity Join our team to drive the implementation of the latest internal and external standards in our Dry Transformers across all the factories in HUB Europe. This role offers you an exciting opportunity to broaden your expertise across various disciplines and functions, engage in dynamic projects and build a strong professional network both within and outside our organization. How you'll make an impact Be an active member of International Standards Committees, staying up to date with emerging changes and requirements and influencing standards development Support Product Management in developing and implementing our Global Technology Platform by engaging in requirements definition and supporting the rollout phases Work with teams from various functions, including Engineering, Operations and Quality. You will establish and maintain both internal and external networks to facilitate information exchange Oversee operations across multiple factories and work towards operational harmonization Ensure the effective implementation of both internal and external standards within our Power Transformers Units in Europe by conducting audits, identifying gaps and facilitating their resolution Responsible for communicating updates, deliver training sessions and provide support and technical guidance to colleagues on Standards Compliance related questions Represent our Company in standards-related discussions with customers and industry stakeholders Your background Master's degree in Engineering (Electrical or Mechanical are preferred) About 10 to 15 years of experience in the Power Transformer field Experience in Project Management and managing functional teams Deep knowledge of transformers design, manufacturing, testing and operations Interest in AI tools and their potential integration into one's work L6S green belt is preferred Excellent intercultural sensitivity and ability to develop and maintain healthy working relationships Strong communication skills, fluent in English, both written and spoken More about us Our flexible working practices help you optimize personal and business performance while creating an environment where all employees can develop their skills and grow.

descriptionByLocale.it: Descrizione Unisciti al nostro team per guidare l'implementazione degli ultimi standard interni ed esterni nei nostri Dry Transformers in tutte le fabbriche di HUB Europe. Questo ruolo vi offre un'opportunità emozionante per ampliare le vostre competenze in diverse discipline e funzioni, impegnarsi in progetti dinamici e costruire una forte rete professionale sia all'interno che all'esterno della nostra organizzazione. ## Mansioni Come farete un impatto - Essere un membro attivo di International Standards Committees, rimanere aggiornati con cambiamenti e requisiti emergenti e influenzare lo sviluppo degli standard - Supporto Product Management nello sviluppo e nell'attuazione della nostra Global Technology Platform, impegnando nella definizione dei requisiti e sostenendo le fasi di rollout - Lavorare con team di varie funzioni, tra cui Ingegneria, Operazioni e Qualità. - No. Istituire e mantenere sia le reti interne che esterne per facilitare lo scambio di informazioni - Superare le operazioni tra più fabbriche e lavorare verso l'armonizzazione operativa - Assicurare l'effettiva implementazione di standard interni ed esterni all'interno delle nostre Power Transformers Units in Europe conducendo audit, identificando le lacune e facilitando la loro risoluzione - Responsabile per la comunicazione di aggiornamenti, fornire sessioni di formazione eccellenti e fornire assistenza tecnica ai colleghi di standard.
descriptionByLocale.en: ## Descrizione The opportunity Join our team to drive the implementation of the latest internal and external standards in our Dry Transformers across all the factories in HUB Europe. This role offers you an exciting opportunity to broaden your expertise across various disciplines and functions, engage in dynamic projects and build a strong professional network both within and outside our organization. ## Mansioni How you'll make an impact - Be an active member of International Standards Committees, staying up to date with emerging changes and requirements and influencing standards development - Support Product Management in developing and implementing our Global Technology Platform by engaging in requirements definition and supporting the rollout phases - Work with teams from various functions, including Engineering, Operations and Quality. - You will establish and maintain both internal and external networks to facilitate information exchange - Oversee operations across multiple factories and work towards operational harmonization - Ensure the effective implementation of both internal and external standards within our Power Transformers Units in Europe by conducting audits, identifying gaps and facilitating their resolution - Responsible for communicating updates, deliver training sessions and provide support and technical guidance to colleagues on Standards Compliance related questions - Represent our Company in standards-related discussions with customers and industry stakeholders ## Requisiti Your background - Master's degree in Engineering (Electrical or Mechanical are preferred) - About 10 to 15 years of experience in the Power Transformer field - Experience in Project Management and managing functional teams - Deep knowledge of transformers design, manufacturing, testing and operations - Interest in AI tools and their potential integration into one's work - L6S green belt is preferred - Excellent intercultural sensitivity and ability to develop and maintain healthy working relationships - Strong communication skills, fluent in English, both written and spoken ## About us - Our flexible working practices help you optimize personal and business performance while creating an environment where all employees can develop their skills and grow.
descriptionByLocale.de: Beschreibung Nehmen Sie an unserem Team teil, um die Umsetzung der neuesten internen und externen Standards in unseren Dry Transformers in allen Fabriken in HUB Europe voranzutreiben. Diese Rolle bietet Ihnen eine spannende Gelegenheit, Ihr Know-how über verschiedene Disziplinen und Funktionen zu erweitern, dynamische Projekte zu engagieren und ein starkes professionelles Netzwerk sowohl innerhalb als auch außerhalb unserer Organisation aufzubauen. In den Warenkorb Wie Sie einen Einfluss haben - Seien Sie aktives Mitglied der International Standards Committees, bleiben Sie auf dem neuesten Stand mit neuen Änderungen und Anforderungen und beeinflussen Standards Entwicklung - Support Product Management bei der Entwicklung und Umsetzung unserer Global Technology Platform durch die Einbeziehung von Anforderungen Definition und Unterstützung der Rollout Phasen - Arbeiten Sie mit Teams aus verschiedenen Funktionen, einschließlich Engineering, Operations und Qualität. - Ja. Sie werden interne und externe Netzwerke einrichten und pflegen, um den Informationsaustausch zu erleichtern - Operationen in mehreren Fabriken zu überwachen und zur operativen Harmonisierung zu arbeiten - Sicherstellung der effektiven Umsetzung von internen und externen Standards in unseren Power Transformers Units in Europa durch Audits, Identifizierung von Lücken und Erleichterung ihrer Auflösung - verantwortlich für die Kommunikation von Updates, Bereitstellung von Schulungen und technische Anleitungen für Kollegen zu Standards Compliance-bezogene Fragen - Repräsentieren Sie unser Unternehmen in standardsbezogenen Diskussionen mit Kunden.
descriptionByLocale.fr: Descrizione L'opportunité Rejoignez notre équipe pour conduire la mise en œuvre des dernières normes internes et externes dans nos transformateurs à sec dans toutes les usines de HUB Europe. Ce rôle vous offre une occasion passionnante d'élargir votre expertise dans diverses disciplines et fonctions, de participer à des projets dynamiques et de bâtir un solide réseau professionnel au sein et à l'extérieur de notre organisation. Les hommes Comment vous allez avoir un impact - Soyez un membre actif des Comités internationaux de normalisation, restez informé des nouveaux changements et exigences et influencez l'élaboration de normes - Supportez la gestion des produits dans le développement et la mise en oeuvre de notre plateforme technologique mondiale en participant à la définition des exigences et en soutenant les phases de déploiement - Travaillez avec des équipes de différentes fonctions, y compris l'ingénierie, les opérations et la qualité. - Oui. Vous établirez et entretenirez des réseaux internes et externes pour faciliter l'échange d'information - Superviser les opérations dans plusieurs usines et travailler à l'harmonisation opérationnelle - Assurer la mise en oeuvre efficace des normes internes et externes au sein de nos unités de transformateurs de puissance en Europe en procédant à des audits, en identifiant les lacunes et en facilitant leur résolution - Responsable de la communication des mises à jour, de la prestation de séances de formation et de l'orientation technique aux collègues sur les questions liées à la conformité - Représenter notre société dans les discussions liées aux normes avec les clients et les intervenants de l'industrie ## Requisi Votre expérience - Maîtrise en génie (on préfère la maîtrise en électronique ou en mécanique) - Environ 10 à 15 ans d'expérience dans le domaine de la transformation de puissance - Expérience en gestion de projet et en gestion d'équipes fonctionnelles - Connaissance approfondie de la conception, de la fabrication, des essais et des opérations des transformateurs - Intérêt pour les outils d'IA et leur intégration potentielle dans son travail - La ceinture verte L6S est préférée - Excellente sensibilité interculturelle et capacité de développer et de maintenir des relations de travail saines - Fortes compétences en communication, couramment en anglais, à la fois écrite.

jobs.title: Senior R&D Reliability Engineer 80 - 100% (f/m/d)
jobs.description: The opportunity Business Unit Service is at the forefront of delivering advanced, reliability-driven service solutions worldwide. As we evolve from descriptive monitoring toward scalable predictive and prescriptive intelligence, reliability modelling becomes a cornerstone of how we create customer value across the asset lifecycle. As Senior Reliability Specialist, you will take an enterprise level role in shaping how reliability intelligence is defined, governed, and applied across our global digital service portfolio. You will operate at the intersection of reliability engineering, digital platforms, and service strategy, ensuring that reliability models are scientifically robust, consistently governed, scalable, and reusable—while being directly connected to operational performance and commercial outcomes. This is a senior, high impact role for an expert who wants to influence how reliability intelligence is used across an entire organization, not just within a single product or project. How you'll make an impact Define and implement the enterprise reliability modelling strategy across BU Service Ensure reliability models deliver consistent, explainable, and decision relevant outputs (e.g., PoF, RUL, risk, mitigation) used in customer facing and decision critical contexts Coordinate companywide projects and activities to harmonize reliability methods and avoid duplication Govern reliability model development, validation, deployment, and retirement Drive integration of reliability models into digital service and predictive platforms Partner with digital, analytics, and IT\/OT teams on data driven reliability solutions Establish standards, guidelines, and training programs for reliability engineering Define and track KPIs and deliver executive insights on reliability performance and maturity Your background Master's or PhD degree in Engineering (Reliability, Systems, Electrical, Mechanical, Industrial, or related) Proven experience in reliability engineering and reliability modelling in mission‑critical environments Strong expertise in failure analysis, reliability modelling, and asset health management methods Experience coordinating or governing technical or modelling activities across multiple teams or units Confidence to work effectively in complex IT\/OT environments with multiple stakeholders Ability to translate reliability insights into operational decisions and service performance outcomes More about us We offer a highly collaborative environment across engineering, digital, analytics, and service teams A senior, visible role shaping the future of reliability intelligence in global digital services Continuous learning through exposure to leading reliability, predictive, and digital service practices Flexible working practices that help you optimize personal and business performance while creating an environment where all employees can develop their skills and grow

You will operate at the intersection of reliability engineering, digital platforms, and service strategy, ensuring that reliability models are scientifically robust, consistently governed, scalable, and reusable—while being directly connected to operational performance and commercial outcomes. This is a senior, high impact role for an expert who wants to influence how reliability intelligence is used across an entire organization, not just within a single product or project. How you'll make an impact Define and implement the enterprise reliability modelling strategy across BU Service Ensure reliability models deliver consistent, explainable, and decision relevant outputs (e.g., PoF, RUL, risk, mitigation) used in customer facing and decision critical contexts Coordinate companywide projects and activities to harmonize reliability methods and avoid duplication Govern reliability model development, validation, deployment, and retirement Drive integration of reliability models into digital service and predictive platforms Partner with digital, analytics, and IT\/OT teams on data driven reliability solutions Establish standards, guidelines, and training programs for reliability engineering Define and track KPIs and deliver executive insights on reliability performance and maturity Your background Master's or PhD degree in Engineering (Reliability, Systems, Electrical, Mechanical, Industrial, or related) Proven experience in reliability engineering and reliability modelling in mission‑critical environments Strong expertise in failure analysis, reliability modelling, and asset health management methods Experience coordinating or governing technical or modelling activities across multiple teams or units Confidence to work effectively in complex IT\/OT environments with multiple stakeholders Ability to translate reliability insights into operational decisions and service performance outcomes More about us We offer a highly collaborative environment across engineering, digital, analytics, and service teams A senior, visible role shaping the future of reliability intelligence in global digital services Continuous learning through exposure to leading reliability, predictive, and digital service practices Flexible working practices that help you optimize personal and business performance while creating an environment where all employees can develop their skills and grow

jobs.title: CNC Dreher/in 80 - 100% (w/m/d)
jobs.description: Über die Position Unsere lokale Produktgruppe Insulation & Components ist technischer Marktführer in der Auslegung und Herstellung von Transformatoren-Durchführungen. Zu den Kunden zählen namhafte, internationale Unternehmen der Elektroindustrie, die sich mit Stromerzeugung, Stromübertragung und Stromverteilung befassen. Unsere Hochspannungs-Durchführungen nehmen weltweit eine Spitzenposition ein. Das kannst Du bewirken Einrichtung und Bedienung von CNC- und konventionellen Drehmaschinen Erstellen der CNC Programme zu den Dreharbeiten Fertigen von Einzelteilen und Kleinserien Pflege und Wartung (VBI) der Maschinen und Anlagen Diverse Aufgaben wie Qualitätsvorgaben einhalten, kontinuierlicher Verbesserungsprozess, konsequente Einhaltung der Arbeitssicherheit Dein Hintergrund Abgeschlossene technische Lehre als Polymechaniker\/in EFZ (oder auch Mechaniker\/in, Maschinenmechaniker\/in) Fundierte Berufserfahrung im Bereich CNC-Drehen, konv. von Vorteil Fliessende Deutschkenntnisse in Wort und Schrift Exakte, ordentliche Arbeitsweise Bereitschaft zum Einsatz im 3 Schicht-Betrieb und flexibler Arbeitszeit Mehr über uns Hitachi Energy steht für innovative Technologien und nachhaltige Energielösungen weltweit. Bei uns erwartet dich ein sicherer Arbeitsplatz in einem zukunftsorientierten Unternehmen, spannende Entwicklungsmöglichkeiten, ein kollegiales und wertschätzendes Arbeitsumfeld sowie attraktive Sozialleistungen.

jobs.title: Power System Analysis and Solution Algorithms Engineer
jobs.description: Job Description The opportunity We are looking for a passionate and proficient industrial specialist with expertise in power system analysis\/ management and high-level programming. In an inter-disciplinary environment, you will drive cutting-edge innovation for different complex systems in power grid applications. Join our dynamic and motivated team to contribute to a sustainable energy future for all. How you'll make an impact You create impact: Together with your team design innovative approaches for grid operation and deliver them as robust software modules You investigate: Translate real power‑system problems into rigorous models and final solutions for the customers You take initiative: Collaborate with a global and inter-disciplinary team of researchers and engineers in Hitachi Energy to drive the development from concept to production You connect: Build relationships within the company and actively share your knowledge and expertise. Interact with local and global external partners including universities, start-ups, and vendors to integrate diverse perspectives and stakeholder inputs You communicate: Be able to clearly communicate concepts to software developers, implementation engineering teams and customers. Produce clear design docs and developer-facing examples\/ notebooks that enable reuse. Your background Master Electrical\/Power Systems Engineering, Automation, Computer Science, Mathematics, or Master's degree with equivalent industry experience Proven expertise in modelling and steady-state analyses of power grids demonstrated hands-on expertise applying these methods in software development. Knowledge in mathematical optimization applied to power systems, and modern numerical solvers is strongly desired (e.g., MIP\/NLP, stochastic; tools such as Gurobi, CPLEX, Ipopt). Experience with machine learning and\/or artificial intelligence technologies is a plus Proficiency in C#, C++, Rust or a similar high-level programming language is required; Python and\/or Matlab for analytics and fast prototyping are a plus. Experience building state-of-the-art software engineering techniques (version control, code reviews, testing, CI\/CD) and integrating solution algorithms into applications are beneficial (e.g., Docker, Kubernetes). High performance computing skills such as parallel\/distributed computing, GPU, OpenMP or MPI would be beneficial Excellent communication skills in English, team player attitude, initiative and creativity More about us We pride ourselves on offering a holistic range of competitive benefits to support your financial, physical and mental wellbeing and personal development. We want you to truly thrive with us, in work and out. We can provide more information during the recruitment process. Are you ready for a new exciting challenge? Does the above description sound like you? Applications will be reviewed on an ongoing basis, so don't delay – apply today! Dbamy o przejrzystość i zgodność z wymogami prawnymi tak, aby każda osoba uczestnicząca w procesie rekrutacyjnym wiedziała, czego może się spodziewać na poszczególnych etapach. Po pomyślnym zakończeniu procesu przedstawimy wybranej osobie szczegóły dotyczące warunków finansowych oraz benefitów. Pozostałe elementy oferty zostaną przekazane przed podpisaniem umowy o pracę, tak by stworzyć możliwość spokojnego zapoznania się z nimi. * Klauzula obowiązuje wyłącznie na terenie Polski , zgodnie z lokalnymi przepisami prawa. We are committed to full transparency and compliance with all legal requirements, ensuring that every candidate knows exactly what to expect at each stage of the recruitment process. Once the process is successfully completed, we will share detailed information about financial conditions and benefits with the selected candidate. Before signing the employment contract, you will receive all remaining details of the offer, giving you the time and space to review everything carefully. *Applicable only for Poland location align with

Knowledge in mathematical optimization applied to power systems, and modern numerical solvers is strongly desired (e.g., MIP\/NLP, stochastic; tools such as Gurobi, CPLEX, Ipopt). Experience with machine learning and\/or artificial intelligence technologies is a plus Proficiency in C#, C++, Rust or a similar high-level programming language is required; Python and\/or Matlab for analytics and fast prototyping are a plus. Experience building state-of-the-art software engineering techniques (version control, code reviews, testing, CI\/CD) and integrating solution algorithms into applications are beneficial (e.g., Docker, Kubernetes). High performance computing skills such as parallel\/distributed computing, GPU, OpenMP or MPI would be beneficial Excellent communication skills in English, team player attitude, initiative and creativity More about us We pride ourselves on offering a holistic range of competitive benefits to support your financial, physical and mental wellbeing and personal development. We want you to truly thrive with us, in work and out. We can provide more information during the recruitment process. Are you ready for a new exciting challenge? Does the above description sound like you? Applications will be reviewed on an ongoing basis, so don't delay – apply today! Dbamy o przejrzystość i zgodność z wymogami prawnymi tak, aby każda osoba uczestnicząca w procesie rekrutacyjnym wiedziała, czego może się spodziewać na poszczególnych etapach. Po pomyślnym zakończeniu procesu przedstawimy wybranej osobie szczegóły dotyczące warunków finansowych oraz benefitów. Pozostałe elementy oferty zostaną przekazane przed podpisaniem umowy o pracę, tak by stworzyć możliwość spokojnego zapoznania się z nimi. * Klauzula obowiązuje wyłącznie na terenie Polski , zgodnie z lokalnymi przepisami prawa. We are committed to full transparency and compliance with all legal requirements, ensuring that every candidate knows exactly what to expect at each stage of the recruitment process. Once the process is successfully completed, we will share detailed information about financial conditions and benefits with the selected candidate. Before signing the employment contract, you will receive all remaining details of the offer, giving you the time and space to review everything carefully. *Applicable only for Poland location align with

jobs.title: Working Student Defectivity Engineering BIMOS 40% (f/m/d)
jobs.description: The opportunity Are you passionate about semiconductors and eager to gain hands-on experience in industry? We are looking for a motivated and detail-oriented student to join our dynamic team for at least 6 months, with the possibility to extend. As a Defectivity Engineering Working Student, you will have the opportunity to work on real-world projects, collaborate with experienced professionals, and contribute to ensuring the highest standards of our semiconductor product quality here in Lenzburg, Aargau. How you'll make an impact Assist in identifying optical defects through non-destructive optical inspection Support defectivity projects through data analysis and reporting on optical yield of our products Support regular maintenance of baseline and Pareto reporting systems Optimize and improve defect portfolio for various products Coordinate with various departments to correlate optical defects with equipment used in manufacturing process Your background Currently enrolled in a Bachelor's or Master's degree program in Materials Science, Engineering, Physics, or a related field Strong teamwork and communication skills Great attention to detail and persistence Fluency in English, German language skills a plus Hands-on mentality and comfort working in a manufacturing environment More about us We are a technology-driven company with a strong focus on innovation, continuous improvement, and sustainable manufacturing. We offer flexible working conditions, dedicated mentorship, and a supportive, inclusive environment where you can grow personally and professionally – empowering you to thrive while balancing life and work.

jobs.title: Installation Project Manager 80 - 100% (f/m/d)
jobs.description: Die Möglichkeit In dieser Funktion übernimmst du am Standort Oerlikon die Organisation, Koordination und Führung aller Montage-, Inbetriebsetzungs- und Instandhaltungsarbeiten vor Ort. Du arbeitest eng mit internen und externen Kunden zusammen und stellst sicher, dass Projekte effizient, sicher und mit hoher Qualität umgesetzt werden. Das kannst du bewirken Verantwortung für die Abwicklung sowie die organisatorische Führung von Arbeiten vor Ort Ansprechperson für interne und externe Kunden Betreuung der zugeteilten Aussendienstmitarbeitenden und Erstellung von Montagezeitplänen unter Berücksichtigung von Ressourcen wie Verbrauchsmaterial, Werkzeug und Personal Überwachung und Dokumentation von Projektfortschritt und - kosten (Reporting) Sicherstellung der Einhaltung von Sicherheitsvorschriften und Qualitätsanforderungen Aktive Minimierung von Projektkosten und Risiken sowie prozessgerechte Bearbeitung von Abweichungsmeldungen inkl. Einleitung von Korrekturmassnahmen Vollständige Zusammenstellung, Ablage und Archivierung aller relevanten Dokumentationen (Red Marks, Prüfprotokolle, Berichte) zur As-Built-Erstellung sowie Verrechnung an den Kunden Das bringst du mit Technische Grundausbildung sowie mehrjährige Erfahrung in der Montage und\/oder Inbetriebnahme von GIS-Anlagen Fundiertes technisches Verständnis; Führungserfahrung von Vorteil Technikerschule \/ Technischer Kaufmann Sehr gute Deutsch- und Englischkenntnisse in Wort und Schrift; weitere Sprachen von Vorteil Sicheres Auftreten und überzeugende Kommunikationsfähigkeiten, insbesondere in der Zusammenarbeit mit unterschiedlichen Kulturen Teamfähigkeit, Organisationsstärke sowie Durchsetzungsvermögen Bereitschaft zu weltweiten Baustellenbesuchen Gute Kenntnisse in SAP und MS Office​ ​Mehr über uns Bei Hitachi Energy setzen wir auf Vielfalt und Zusammenarbeit. Unterschiedliche Perspektiven stärken unsere Innovation und ermöglichen Lösungen, die unsere Energiezukunft nachhaltig prägen. Wenn du Teil eines globalen Teams sein möchtest, das gemeinsam Wirkung erzielt, freuen wir uns auf deine Bewerbung.

jobs.title: Global AI Prompting Engineer PGTR
jobs.description: The opportunity As part of our ongoing digital transformation journey, we are developing ACE, a cutting-edge Generative AI-powered platform designed to enhance the efficiency and accuracy of our Bid & Proposal Management process within the Transformers Business Unit. We are seeking a Prompt Engineer who will play a pivotal role in this initiative. This individual will be responsible for designing, testing, and optimizing domain-specific prompts that enable ACE to generate accurate, context-aware, and business-aligned outputs. The role requires a deep understanding of transformer industry, industrial equipment standards as well as basic understanding of prompt engineering best practices and GenAI model behavior, ensuring that ACE delivers high-quality responses tailored to complex business scenarios. The ideal candidate will collaborate closely with IT Experts, Subject Matter Experts, Business stakeholders, and Module Owners to break down complex business logic into domain-specific prompts. How you'll make an impact Develop, refine, and maintain prompt libraries for various transformer units\/factories. Implement advanced techniques like prompt chaining, context injection, and few-shot learning for enterprise-grade accuracy. Own prompt lifecycle management including versioning, traceability, and governance. Evaluate LLM responses for precision, compliance, and bias mitigation. Conduct A\/B testing and maintain performance dashboards for prompt effectiveness. Work closely with Module Owners, Subject Matter Experts, and Business Stakeholders to embed prompts into ACE workflows. Create clear documentation for prompt strategies and maintain best practices aligned with Hitachi Energy's AI Ethics Charter. Stay updated on emerging GenAI technologies, frameworks, and tools to enhance ACE capabilities. Implement and recommend best practices for prompting across different ACE modules. Your background Bachelor or master degree in electrical engineering Experience working in the transformer industry, with international standards and customer documentation. Strong problem-solving and analytical thinking. Strong linguistic and logical reasoning for crafting precise prompts. Strong understanding of NLP, context management, and token optimization. Ability to experiment and iterate to optimize outputs. Ability to understand business processes and translate them into ACE-specific prompts. Hands-on experience with LLMs and prompt engineering techniques. Strong interest in AI enterprise-grade solutions. Willingness to learn Python and experience with AI APIs services. Ability to work in Agile environments and manage multiple stakeholders. Excellent communication and collaboration skills. Analytical mindset with attention to detail. Empathy for end users and curiosity for experimentation. Commitment to ethical AI practices and continuous learning. Reduction in prompt failure rate and model hallucination. Improvement in accuracy of outputs by modules. Speed and scalability of prompt deployment across ACE modules within the Transformer Business Unit. Compliance with AI governance and security standards. More about us Be part of a global digital transformation initiative. Work on cutting-edge GenAI solutions. Collaborative culture with opportunities for career growth and innovation. We are committed to full transparency and compliance with all legal requirements, ensuring that every candidate knows exactly what to expect at each stage of the recruitment process. Once the process is successfully completed, we will share detailed information about financial conditions and benefits with the selected candidate. Before signing the employment contract, you will receive all remaining details of the offer, giving you the time and space to review everything carefully. * * Applicable only for Poland location align with local law regulations

Stay updated on emerging GenAI technologies, frameworks, and tools to enhance ACE capabilities. Implement and recommend best practices for prompting across different ACE modules. Your background Bachelor or master degree in electrical engineering Experience working in the transformer industry, with international standards and customer documentation. Strong problem-solving and analytical thinking. Strong linguistic and logical reasoning for crafting precise prompts. Strong understanding of NLP, context management, and token optimization. Ability to experiment and iterate to optimize outputs. Ability to understand business processes and translate them into ACE-specific prompts. Hands-on experience with LLMs and prompt engineering techniques. Strong interest in AI enterprise-grade solutions. Willingness to learn Python and experience with AI APIs services. Ability to work in Agile environments and manage multiple stakeholders. Excellent communication and collaboration skills. Analytical mindset with attention to detail. Empathy for end users and curiosity for experimentation. Commitment to ethical AI practices and continuous learning. Reduction in prompt failure rate and model hallucination. Improvement in accuracy of outputs by modules. Speed and scalability of prompt deployment across ACE modules within the Transformer Business Unit. Compliance with AI governance and security standards. More about us Be part of a global digital transformation initiative. Work on cutting-edge GenAI solutions. Collaborative culture with opportunities for career growth and innovation. We are committed to full transparency and compliance with all legal requirements, ensuring that every candidate knows exactly what to expect at each stage of the recruitment process. Once the process is successfully completed, we will share detailed information about financial conditions and benefits with the selected candidate. Before signing the employment contract, you will receive all remaining details of the offer, giving you the time and space to review everything carefully. * * Applicable only for Poland location align with local law regulations
descriptionByLocale.fr: The opportunity As part of our ongoing digital transformation journey, we are developing ACE, a cutting-edge Generative AI-powered platform designed to enhance the efficiency and accuracy of our Bid & Proposal Management process within the Transformers Business Unit. We are seeking a Prompt Engineer who will play a pivotal role in this initiative. This individual will be responsible for designing, testing, and optimizing domain-specific prompts that enable ACE to generate accurate, context-aware, and business-aligned outputs. The role requires a deep understanding of transformer industry, industrial equipment standards as well as basic understanding of prompt engineering best practices and GenAI model behavior, ensuring that ACE delivers high-quality responses tailored to complex business scenarios. The ideal candidate will collaborate closely with IT Experts, Subject Matter Experts, Business stakeholders, and Module Owners to break down complex business logic into domain-specific prompts. How you'll make an impact Develop, refine, and maintain prompt libraries for various transformer units\/factories. Implement advanced techniques like prompt chaining, context injection, and few-shot learning for enterprise-grade accuracy. Own prompt lifecycle management including versioning, traceability, and governance. Evaluate LLM responses for precision, compliance, and bias mitigation. Conduct A\/B testing and maintain performance dashboards for prompt effectiveness. Work closely with Module Owners, Subject Matter Experts, and Business Stakeholders to embed prompts into ACE workflows. Create clear documentation for prompt strategies and maintain best practices aligned with Hitachi Energy's AI Ethics Charter. Stay updated on emerging GenAI technologies, frameworks, and tools to enhance ACE capabilities. Implement and recommend best practices for prompting across different ACE modules. Your background Bachelor or master degree in electrical engineering Experience working in the transformer industry, with international standards and customer documentation. Strong problem-solving and analytical thinking. Strong linguistic and logical reasoning for crafting precise prompts. Strong understanding of NLP, context management, and token optimization. Ability to experiment and iterate to optimize outputs. Ability to understand business processes and translate them into ACE-specific prompts. Hands-on experience with LLMs and prompt engineering techniques. Strong interest in AI enterprise-grade solutions. Willingness to learn Python and experience with AI APIs services. Ability to work in Agile environments and manage multiple stakeholders. Excellent communication and collaboration skills. Analytical mindset with attention to detail. Empathy for end users and curiosity for experimentation. Commitment to ethical AI practices and continuous learning. Reduction in prompt failure rate and model hallucination. Improvement in accuracy of outputs by modules. Speed and scalability of prompt deployment across ACE modules within the Transformer Business Unit. Compliance with AI governance and security standards. More about us Be part of a global digital transformation initiative. Work on cutting-edge GenAI solutions. Collaborative culture with opportunities for career growth and innovation. We are committed to full transparency and compliance with all legal requirements, ensuring that every candidate knows exactly what to expect at each stage of the recruitment process. jobs.title: Incoming Quality Inspector 80 - 100% (f/m/d)
jobs.description: Über die Position In deiner vielseitigen Rolle als Incoming Quality Inspector arbeitest du in einem kleinen Team an unserem Standort in Dietlikon und bist mit diesem zusammen verantwortlich, dass unsere internationalen Kunden weiterhin mit den höchsten Qualitätsstandards und einem reibungslosen Betrieb ihrer Anlagen rechnen können. Du bist ein ausgewiesener Teamplayer und kannst anpacken? Du möchtest uns helfen, unsere ambitionierten Ziele für die Zukunft zu erreichen und zusammen mit uns das Service Logistics Center stetig weiter zu entwickeln? – Dann bewirb dich jetzt! Wir bieten dir eine interessante und abwechslungsreiche Aufgabe in einem herausfordernden Umfeld. Das kannst du bewirken Qualitative und Quantitative Prüfung der Materialeingänge mittels mechanischer, elektrischer und optischer Messmittel sowie der dazugehörigen Dokumente Leiten \/ Unterstützen von Aufgaben bei Qualitätsabweichungen Erfassen und verfolgen von Qualitätsabweichungen in SAP Schnittstellenübergreifende Zusammenarbeit mit verschiedenen Teams wie Einkauf, Technik oder Auftragsabwicklung Stetiges verbessern unserer Qualität durch interne Prozessverbesserungen Umsetzung der 5S-Prinzipien Vorleben der Qualitätskultur als Vorbild Das bringst du mit Abgeschlossene technische Berufsausbildung, z.B. Polymechanik, Industriemechanik, oder vergleichbare Ausbildung Sehr gutes Verständnis von technischen Zeichnungen, Messmittel, Material und Stücklisten Gute Kenntnisse in Microsoft Office sowie idealerweise Erfahrung im Umgang mit SAP Gute Deutschkenntnisse und Englischkenntnisse Hohe Leistungsbereitschaft und Motivation um den Standort im Team weiterzuentwickeln Lösungsorientiert und pragmatisch mit Sinn für Prozesse und übergeordnete Zusammenhänge Spass an Teamarbeit und gemeinsamer Erreichung von hochgesteckten Zielen Einwandfreier Leumund Mehr über uns Unsere flexiblen Arbeitsmethoden helfen dir, deine persönlichen und beruflichen Leistungen zu optimieren, und schaffen ein Umfeld, in dem alle Mitarbeiter ihre Fähigkeiten entwickeln und wachsen können.

jobs.title: Teamleiter Instandhaltung 80 – 100% (f/m/d)
jobs.description: Über die Position In unserem Profit Center Überspannungsableiter in Wettingen produzieren und offerieren wir innovative, zuverlässige und sichere Überspannungsschutzlösungen für die Segmente Stromerzeugung\/-verteilung sowie Railway und Industrie. Unser Anspruch dabei ist, dass wir unsere Kunden auf der ganzen Welt als Technologieführer mit Überspannungsschutzlösungen auf Weltniveau begeistern. Dies erreichen wir durch den stetigen Ausbau unserer Führerschaft in Kerntechnologien wie auch durch die ständige Verbesserung unserer Geschäftsprozesse. Als Teamleiter Instandhaltung führen sie ein Team von 6 Personen tragen Sie die Gesamtverantwortung für die Instandhaltung aller Produktionsanlagen und technischen Systeme in einer hochautomatisierten Prozessfertigungsumgebung. Ziel der Funktion ist die Sicherstellung einer hohen Anlagenverfügbarkeit, maximaler Sicherheit sowie einer effizienten und kostenoptimierten Instandhaltungsorganisation. Sie agieren als strategischer Partner der Geschäftsleitung und führen die Instandhaltung sowohl strategisch als auch operativ. Das können Sie bewirken Strategische und operative Führung der Instandhaltungsorganisation Planung, Steuerung und Umsetzung von Wartungs- und Instandhaltungsmaßnahmen (präventiv, korrektiv und ganzheitliche Instandhaltungskonzepte) Entwicklung und Umsetzung von Instandhaltungsstrategien zur Steigerung von Anlagenverfügbarkeit, Produktivität und Anlagenlebensdauer Fachliche und disziplinarische Führung sowie Weiterentwicklung des Instandhaltungsteams Verantwortung für Budgetplanung, Kostenkontrolle sowie Ersatzteil- und Lagerstrategie Unterstützung von Umbau-, Neubau- und Investitionsprojekten Sicherstellung der Einhaltung von Sicherheits-, Umwelt- und gesetzlichen Vorgaben Kontinuierliche Verbesserung der Instandhaltungsprozesse sowie Einführung digitaler Tools und Kennzahlensysteme Das bringen Sie mit Abgeschlossenes technisches Studium im Bereich Ingenieurwesen, Mechatronik, Elektrotechnik, Automatisierungstechnik oder vergleichbare Qualifikation Mehrjährige Führungs- und Instandhaltungserfahrung in einer Prozess- oder Serienfertigung mit hoher Automatisierung Fundierte Kenntnisse in Automatisierungs-, Roboter- und industriellen Steuerungssystemen Erfahrung mit strukturierten Instandhaltungsansätzen, kontinuierlicher Verbesserung und zustandsorientierter Wartung Erfahrung in der Zusammenarbeit mit interdisziplinären Schnittstellen sowie im Management technischer Investitionsprojekte Analytische, strukturierte und umsetzungsstarke Arbeitsweise mit ausgeprägter Führungskompetenz Sehr gute Deutsch- und Englischkenntnisse Mehr über uns Unsere flexiblen Arbeitsmethoden helfen Ihnen, Ihre persönlichen und beruflichen Leistungen zu optimieren, und schaffen ein Umfeld, in dem alle Mitarbeiter ihre Fähigkeiten entwickeln und wachsen können.

Das können Sie bewirken Strategische und operative Führung der Instandhaltungsorganisation Planung, Steuerung und Umsetzung von Wartungs- und Instandhaltungsmaßnahmen (präventiv, korrektiv und ganzheitliche Instandhaltungskonzepte) Entwicklung und Umsetzung von Instandhaltungsstrategien zur Steigerung von Anlagenverfügbarkeit, Produktivität und Anlagenlebensdauer Fachliche und disziplinarische Führung sowie Weiterentwicklung des Instandhaltungsteams Verantwortung für Budgetplanung, Kostenkontrolle sowie Ersatzteil- und Lagerstrategie Unterstützung von Umbau-, Neubau- und Investitionsprojekten Sicherstellung der Einhaltung von Sicherheits-, Umwelt- und gesetzlichen Vorgaben Kontinuierliche Verbesserung der Instandhaltungsprozesse sowie Einführung digitaler Tools und Kennzahlensysteme Das bringen Sie mit Abgeschlossenes technisches Studium im Bereich Ingenieurwesen, Mechatronik, Elektrotechnik, Automatisierungstechnik oder vergleichbare Qualifikation Mehrjährige Führungs- und Instandhaltungserfahrung in einer Prozess- oder Serienfertigung mit hoher Automatisierung Fundierte Kenntnisse in Automatisierungs-, Roboter- und industriellen Steuerungssystemen Erfahrung mit strukturierten Instandhaltungsansätzen, kontinuierlicher Verbesserung und zustandsorientierter Wartung Erfahrung in der Zusammenarbeit mit interdisziplinären Schnittstellen sowie im Management technischer Investitionsprojekte Analytische, strukturierte und umsetzungsstarke Arbeitsweise mit ausgeprägter Führungskompetenz Sehr gute Deutsch- und Englischkenntnisse Mehr über uns Unsere flexiblen Arbeitsmethoden helfen Ihnen, Ihre persönlichen und beruflichen Leistungen zu optimieren, und schaffen ein Umfeld, in dem alle Mitarbeiter ihre Fähigkeiten entwickeln und wachsen können.
descriptionByLocale.fr: Über die Position In unserem Profit Center Überspannungsableiter in Wettingen produzieren und offerieren wir innovative, zuverlässige und sichere Überspannungsschutzlösungen für die Segmente Stromerzeugung\/-verteilung sowie Railway und Industrie. Unser Anspruch dabei ist, dass wir unsere Kunden auf der ganzen Welt als Technologieführer mit Überspannungsschutzlösungen auf Weltniveau begeistern. Dies erreichen wir durch den stetigen Ausbau unserer Führerschaft in Kerntechnologien wie auch durch die ständige Verbesserung unserer Geschäftsprozesse. Als Teamleiter Instandhaltung führen sie ein Team von 6 Personen tragen Sie die Gesamtverantwortung für die Instandhaltung aller Produktionsanlagen und technischen Systeme in einer hochautomatisierten Prozessfertigungsumgebung. Ziel der Funktion ist die Sicherstellung einer hohen Anlagenverfügbarkeit, maximaler Sicherheit sowie einer effizienten und kostenoptimierten Instandhaltungsorganisation. Sie agieren als strategischer Partner der Geschäftsleitung und führen die Instandhaltung sowohl strategisch als auch operativ. jobs.title: Global IT Manager for Business Unit Grid Automation
jobs.description: The opportunity The Grid Automation Business Unit of Hitachi Energy is seeking a dynamic Global IT Leader for the role of Global BST Manager, Grid Automation, to lead an existing team of over forty IT Professionals in supporting and advancing Grid Automation's global businesses and digital IT transformation initiatives. Business Solution Teams (BSTs) in each Business Unit (BU) have complete responsibility for managing BU-specific applications and Infrastructure services, including service delivery. They oversee and control these applications and services while adhering to global governance and security standards. BSTs design and implement the IT Roadmap and Application Landscape for their respective BUs to align with business requirements. They closely collaborate with global departments to help deliver function-wide applications and enterprise platforms. How you will make an impact Lead Business relationship management with BUs including BST Leadership and guidance across BU\/HUB\/GPG\/Functions. Lead IT Demand Mgt to meet Business requirements. Management and governance of the BST IT organization to support and deliver on Demand priorities. Accountable for Application Management for BU specific applications, Infrastructure\/ Onsite Service Delivery Operational Management of BST assigned Sites. Support IT\/OT Cybersecurity Onsite Service Delivery Operational Management of BST assigned Sites. Digital Transformation IT Build Deployments to support growth of business. Accountable for the total IT offering to the business and provide interface to relevant IT partners, articulating the needs of the business to the relevant IT partners to ensure impactful support to the business. Ensure alignment of IT roadmap to business needs through communication and active feedback loops between stakeholders and IT organization. Define and implement strategic IT priorities in the organization in line with BU strategy and Strategic Business initiatives, covering business functions application landscape, utilizing the existing IT solutions portfolio or ensuring to provide new solutions, where appropriate. Understand the business demands, the current and long-term business strategy and translate them into opportunities for IT to add value to the business. Implement and ensure compliance with global and local standards, tools, processes related to operations\/projects execution. Analyze results of internal and external audits and identify common areas of improvement within the organization. Provide the BU escalation point for all IT related issues. Responsible for BST budget and investments and creating transparency on business unit IT costs. Setting standard documentation for audits and their records, findings and reports. Develop and share best practices across BST community. Foster communication between BU and IT organization. Ensure (with HR support) that the area of responsibility is properly organized, staffed, skilled and directed. Guides, motivates and develops direct and indirect subordinates within HR policies, for the benefit of both company and the employee. Your background Minimum 15+ years of professional working experience in IT. 10+ years' experience working in global and diverse organizations with understanding of Infrastructure, Applications and platforms. A bachelor's degree is required; a master's degree is preferred. Strong written and verbal communication skills. Highly organized with attention to detail. Proven success working in matrixed organizations. Capable of successfully leading remote teams across multiple time zones More about us: Hitachi Energy is a global technology leader that is advancing a sustainable energy future for all. We serve customers in the utility, industry and infrastructure sectors with innovative solutions and services across the value chain. Together with customers and partners, we pioneer technologies and enable the digital transformation required to accelerate the energy transition towards

jobs.title: Global BU Sales Leader - Grid Automation
jobs.description: Join Hitachi Energy's Grid Automation business as our Global Head of Sales. You will lead a global team driving commercial strategy and sales execution across a portfolio that includes enterprise software, automation and communications, power conversion solutions and services. This is a high-impact leadership role at the center of our global go-to-market transformation (Suisen)—focused on customer connection, agility, empowerment, and commercial excellence. You will work across product lines and regions to align diverse sales teams and deliver growth, while shaping how we serve customers in a rapidly evolving energy landscape. The location of this role is flexible globally and will be determined in subsequent stages. How You'll Make an Impact Lead global sales performance across all Grid Automation offerings. Manage a small central team of industry leaders and functional experts focused on global sales strategy and governance. Provide dotted-line leadership to sales leaders in global product groups and regional hubs. Align multiple sales operating models under a unified go-to-market strategy. Drive forecasting discipline, pipeline health, and deal governance. Champion customer-first sales execution and strategic account leadership. Accelerate growth in software, digital, and lifecycle-based offerings. Expand reach through partner ecosystems and channel programs. Collaborate with regional, product, and functional leaders to ensure execution. Lead through transformation, simplifying structures and empowering teams. Your Background 15+ years in global sales leadership roles in energy, software, or industrial sectors. Proven success managing complex, matrixed sales organizations. Experience leading sales transformation or go-to-market redesigns. Strong commercial acumen and forecasting discipline. Familiarity with enterprise software, automation, and power systems. Executive presence and ability to influence across functions and geographies. Bachelor's degree required; advanced degree preferred. Fluent in English; additional languages are a plus.

jobs.title: Technical Buyer 80 - 100% (f/m/d)
jobs.description: The opportunity Hitachi Energy, High Voltage Products, is renowned worldwide as a specialist for power transmission and distribution. The Service unit is responsible for the worldwide after sales support of generator circuit-breaker (GCB) systems and gas insulated switchgears (GIS), including training, installation and commissioning, scheduled maintenance, added value services, troubleshooting, extensions, retrofits and replacements. As a Technical Buyer, you act as a key interface between Procurement, Engineering, Quality, and our global supplier network. You ensure that technically specified materials, components, and services are clearly defined, technically clarified, qualified, and available in line with project, quality, and delivery requirements. Through close cross‑functional collaboration, you translate engineering requirements into robust sourcing solutions and support supplier qualification and technical alignment. Your role contributes directly to product quality, supply reliability, and operational excellence, enabling Hitachi Energy to deliver innovative and sustainable energy solutions to our customers worldwide. How you'll make an impact Perform technical clarification of materials, drawings, specifications, and engineering changes in close collaboration with Engineering and suppliers Support and coordinate Production Part Approval Process (PPAP) activities, first samples, and supplier qualification processes Conduct technical evaluations of alternative materials, deviation requests, and obsolescence cases Collaborate closely with Strategic and Operational Buyers on supplier- and material-related topics Support the handling of supplier complaints, Non‑Conformity Reports (NCRs), and root cause analyses Support and participate in supplier assessments and technical supplier evaluations, including capability, quality, and process maturity reviews Contribute to supplier performance evaluations together with Quality and Strategic Procurement Ensure accuracy and completeness of material and supplier master data in SAP and act as the technical contact point during supplier escalations Support Service Organization and Project Teams in complex or urgent procurement topics Your background Completed technical education (e.g. Polymechanic, Automation Technician, Mechanical Technician, Design Engineer or similar) 3-5 years experience in technical procurement or SCM‑related roles Strong understanding of technical drawings, Bill of Material and manufacturing processes Experience working with suppliers on technical and quality‑related topics Structured, solution‑oriented, and communicative working style Good German skills and very good English skills SAP knowledge Proactive, structured, and solution-focused working style. Team oriented mindset with strong cross-functional collaboration More about us Our flexible working practices help you optimize personal and business performance while creating an environment where all employees can develop their skills and grow.

descriptionByLocale.it: The opportunity Hitachi Energy, High Voltage Products, is renowned worldwide as a specialist for power transmission and distribution. The Service unit is responsible for the worldwide after sales support of generator circuit-breaker (GCB) systems and gas insulated switchgears (GIS), including training, installation and commissioning, scheduled maintenance, added value services, troubleshooting, extensions, retrofits and replacements. As a Technical Buyer, you act as a key interface between Procurement, Engineering, Quality, and our global supplier network. You ensure that technically specified materials, components, and services are clearly defined, technically clarified, qualified, and available in line with project, quality, and delivery requirements. Through close cross‑functional collaboration, you translate engineering requirements into robust sourcing solutions and support supplier qualification and technical alignment. Your role contributes directly to product quality, supply reliability, and operational excellence, enabling Hitachi Energy to deliver innovative and sustainable energy solutions to our customers worldwide. How you'll make an impact Perform technical clarification of materials, drawings, specifications, and engineering changes in close collaboration with Engineering and suppliers Support and coordinate Production Part Approval Process (PPAP) activities, first samples, and supplier qualification processes Conduct technical evaluations of alternative materials, deviation requests, and obsolescence cases Collaborate closely with Strategic and Operational Buyers on supplier- and material-related topics Support the handling of supplier complaints, Non‑Conformity Reports (NCRs), and root cause analyses Support and participate in supplier assessments and technical supplier evaluations, including capability, quality, and process maturity reviews Contribute to supplier performance evaluations together with Quality and Strategic Procurement Ensure accuracy and completeness of material and supplier master data in SAP and act as the technical contact point during supplier escalations Support Service Organization and Project Teams in complex or urgent procurement topics Your background Completed technical education (e.g. Polymechanic, Automation Technician, Mechanical Technician, Design Engineer or similar) 3-5 years experience in technical procurement or SCM‑related roles Strong understanding of technical drawings, Bill of Material and manufacturing processes Experience working with suppliers on technical and quality‑related topics Structured, solution‑oriented, and communicative working style Good German skills and very good English skills SAP knowledge Proactive, structured, and solution-focused working style. Team oriented mindset with strong cross-functional collaboration More about us Our flexible working practices help you optimize personal and business performance while creating an environment where all employees can develop their skills and grow.

jobs.title: Mitarbeiter/in Endprüfung 80 - 100% (w/m/d)
jobs.description: Über die Position Als Mitarbeiter Endprüfung bei Hitachi Energy bist du ein entscheidendes Bindeglied zwischen Produktion und Auslieferung. Du stellst sicher, dass unsere Produkte vor der Freigabe höchsten Qualitäts-, Sicherheits- und Dokumentationsanforderungen entsprechen. Mit deinem Qualitätsbewusstsein und deinem strukturierten Vorgehen trägst du maßgeblich zur Zuverlässigkeit unserer Produkte und zur Zufriedenheit unserer Kunden bei. Das kannst du bewirken Testen von Generatorleistungsschaltern im Prüffeld (80%): Prüfen der Generator Leistungsschalter gemäss vorgegebenem Prüfplan Bewertung der Messergebnisse Durchführung von Kundenabnahmen in Englischer Sprache Erkennen von mechanischen und elektrischen Fehlern Analyse und Korrektur von Verdrahtungsfehlern im Steuerschrank Übernahme der Verantwortung für das Prüfen der zugeteilten Schalter (zeitlicher Ablauf sowie Qualität der Prüfung) Mitarbeit bei der Verbesserung des Messequipments sowie der Prüfprozesse Montage- und Verdrahtungsarbeiten (20%): Selbstständige Verdrahtung von Baugruppen und Schaltersystemen gemäss Arbeitspapieren wie Kundenauftrag, Elektroschema, Checkliste und Arbeits­plan Montage von PGHV-Produkten entsprechend der in den Qualitätssicherungsverfahren und Arbeitsanweisungen festgehaltenen Vorgaben und Zielsetzungen Lesen und verstehen von technischen Zeichnungen und Stücklisten Das bringst du mit Abgeschlossene elektrotechnische Grundausbildung (Automatiker EFZ, o.ä.) Erfahrung im lesen und verstehen von elektrischen Schemas, Montagezeichnungen und Arbeitsanweisungen Kenntnisse der Prüfung elektrischer Maschinen und von Steuerschränken sind von Vorteil Sehr gute Deutsch- und Englischkenntnisse (mündlich, schriftlich) Affinität zu Computern (Messequipment, diverse Softwaretools, Dokumentation erstellen und pflegen) Gutes Verständnis für Arbeitssicherheit, hohes Qualitätsbewusstsein sowie kundenorientiertes Denken und professioneller Umgang mit Kunden Mehr über uns Hitachi Energy steht für innovative Technologien und nachhaltige Energielösungen weltweit. Bei uns erwartet dich ein sicherer Arbeitsplatz in einem zukunftsorientierten Unternehmen, spannende Entwicklungsmöglichkeiten, ein kollegiales und wertschätzendes Arbeitsumfeld sowie attraktive Sozialleistungen.

jobs.title: Global Product Specialist – Solid-State Transformers (SSTs) 80 – 100% (f/m/d)
jobs.description: The opportunity Solid-State Transformers (SSTs) are emerging as critical infrastructure for modern data centers, particularly AI facilities, by providing high efficiency (up to 98.5%), compact, and intelligent power management. Unlike conventional transformers, SSTs utilize power electronics to convert medium-voltage grid power directly to 800V DC, significantly reducing footprint, increasing efficiency, and easing grid interconnection. Join us as a Global Product Specialist – Solid-State Transformers (SSTs) and play a pivotal role in shaping the future of data center supply infrastructure, guiding both Hitachi Energy and clients toward smarter, greener, and more resilient operations. If you combine a solid understanding of relevant technologies with insight into customer value drivers—and can translate customer needs into actionable requirements that keep our solutions both innovative and aligned with client expectations—then this role is an excellent fit for you. How you´ll make an impact Conduct market evaluations, competitor analyses, and identify gaps in product, solutions, and services, while assessing Hitachi Energy's positioning Gather and analyze global standards, market requirements, customer needs, and values to contribute to the product roadmap Support the Global Product Manager in creating business cases for product, solutions, and service development projects Support global and local marketing teams in creating market communication materials, including assistance with new product launches Provide sales support, participate in customer meetings, and attend trade shows as required, ensuring the availability of appropriate training materials for internal and external use Drive technical solutions across Hitachi Energy's global footprint, following an annual cycle of activities to govern Global Product Management Your background Bachelor's degree in engineering, along with 10+ years of related experience preferred Prior experience in Product Management or Product Ownership within data centre domain, and knowledge of Solid-State Transformer (SST) technology Possess a technical mindset to serve as a technical expert, instilling customer confidence in the product and contributing to product design Ability to simplify and present the value proposition of a complex offering Strong expertise in qualitative and quantitative business analysis Proficient in analyzing market potential through product segmentation, go-to-market channels, and developing business cases. Willingness to travel domestically and internationally (20%) More about us Our flexible working practices help you optimize personal and business performance while creating an environment where all employees can develop their skills and grow.

How you´ll make an impact Conduct market evaluations, competitor analyses, and identify gaps in product, solutions, and services, while assessing Hitachi Energy's positioning Gather and analyze global standards, market requirements, customer needs, and values to contribute to the product roadmap Support the Global Product Manager in creating business cases for product, solutions, and service development projects Support global and local marketing teams in creating market communication materials, including assistance with new product launches Provide sales support, participate in customer meetings, and attend trade shows as required, ensuring the availability of appropriate training materials for internal and external use Drive technical solutions across Hitachi Energy's global footprint, following an annual cycle of activities to govern Global Product Management Your background Bachelor's degree in engineering, along with 10+ years of related experience preferred Prior experience in Product Management or Product Ownership within data centre domain, and knowledge of Solid-State Transformer (SST) technology Possess a technical mindset to serve as a technical expert, instilling customer confidence in the product and contributing to product design Ability to simplify and present the value proposition of a complex offering Strong expertise in qualitative and quantitative business analysis Proficient in analyzing market potential through product segmentation, go-to-market channels, and developing business cases. Willingness to travel domestically and internationally (20%) More about us Our flexible working practices help you optimize personal and business performance while creating an environment where all employees can develop their skills and grow.

jobs.title: Legal Counsel 80 - 100% (f/m/d)
jobs.description: As Legal Counsel, you will play a key role in our legal team by providing comprehensive legal support to several Business Units and central Functions. As a generalist you cover all fields of contract and commercial law, with an additional focus on public procurement law, R&D, labour law, and data privacy. Your responsibilities will encompass the management and execution of all types of contracts, overseeing potential disputes, and handling various day-to-day business operations. If you possess refined legal skills, can tackle complex issues within an international corporate setting, and are determined, energetic, and capable of influencing at all levels, we invite you to join our team! How you will make an impact: Comprehensive legal support to Hitachi Energy's Business Units and central Functions in all domestic and international legal matters, in particular in the field of contract and commercial law, with an additional focus on public procurement, R&D, Labour Law, and Data Privacy Offer targeted legal advice to support key strategic goals, safeguard the company's reputation and interests, and ensure compliance with relevant laws, regulations and internal policies Communicate and work cross-functionally with other legal counsels, and your stakeholders namely in Sales, SCM, Research, and Management ﻿﻿Provide training to internal clients on relevant legal and contractual topics ﻿﻿Develop and implement standards, policies, and procedures related to your function Advise on the resolution of pre-litigation claims and disputes and handle complex commercial litigation mat-ters, including selecting, engaging, and managing outside counsel, and collaborating with Hitachi Energy local counsels Your background: Master's degree in law, preferably with additional qualifications acquired in the US or UK Admission to the Bar in Switzerland Legal generalist with experience as in-house counsel or attorney-at-law in contract and commercial law. Knowledge in R&D, Labour Law, and Data Privacy is a plus Business- and solution-driven approach with yet the ability to provide accurate, high-quality legal advice and a sound judgment in developing and implementing legal solutions Responsiveness and high degree of responsibility with the willingness to think “out of the box” Ability to work under pressure and in a cross-functional and cross-cultural environment that is changing and may be challenging Well-developed oral and written communication skills in German and English; French and Italian are an advantage More about us : At Hitachi Energy, we believe that diversity powers innovation and collaboration. Our teams are made up of individuals from a wide range of backgrounds, experiences, and perspectives, working together across borders and disciplines to shape a more sustainable energy future. We are committed to fostering an inclusive environment where everyone feels respected, valued, and empowered to contribute their best.

jobs.title: Laboratory Manager – High Voltage Testing 80 - 100% (f/m/d)
jobs.description: The opportunity Our local product group Insulation & Components is the technology leader in design and manufacture of high volt-age transformer bushings. All renowned international companies of the electrical industry, active in power genera-tion, transmission and distribution are among our customers. We have a top position in the high voltage trans-former bushing areas worldwide. In this highly responsible and dynamic role, you will lead our laboratory team at MICAFIL in Zürich-Altstetten (ZH) and ensure that we further strengthen our competitiveness and develop our Swiss branch to meet the global market growth in the field of energy. In this important role, you will continuously drive forward the efficient and innovative processes of our 3 different laboratories together with a strong team. If you have a passion for leadership and high voltage testing, we offer you the stage to bring about real change and actively shape the future of our production processes. How you'll make an impact Lead the laboratory team, including resource management and team development (15 FTE) and act as the point of contact for HV testing at Zürich-Altstetten MICAFIL Ensure compliance with safety and quality standards (IEC\/IEEE) Analyze & implement efficient production & business processes to increase safety, quality and productivity Ensure high output through reliable high-voltage testing procedures (AC, DC, impulse, partial discharge) Identify and integrate new technologies and best practices to drive the continuous improvement process (CIP) together with your team and our Process Engineering team Collaborate closely with planning, quality assurance, logistics, other production departments and relevant stakeholders for holistic process design and improvement Be responsible for optimal work and maintenance instructions and other relevant regulations (e.g. ISO, Lean, Six Sigma) Develop and monitor the most important key performance indicators (KPIs) to measure success and re-port to operations manager As People Manager, you will be responsible to ensure compliance with applicable external and internal regulations, procedures and guidelines Your background Technical apprenticeship and qualifications (Bachelor \/ Master) in the field of Electrical Engineering or equivalent Several years of experience in a similar leadership position (High-Voltage Testing\/Insulation Technology\/Power Engineering) Solid knowledge in high-voltage systems, generators, and automation systems Solid knowledge of lean management, Six Sigma and other process optimization methods Proven experience in leading and motivating cross-functional teams Strong problem-solving skills and a data-driven approach to decision making combined with a strong focus on safety Excellent time management, communication and organizational skills Fluent in German and English More about us Our flexible working practices help you optimize personal and business performance while creating an environment where all employees can develop their skills and grow.

jobs.title: Praktikant Praxisintegriertes Bachelorstudium (PiBs) Wirtschaftsingenieurwesen 40% (m/w/d)
jobs.description: Über die Position Starte deine Karriere mit einem praxisnahen Einstieg in die Industrie und gestalte unsere Produktion durch datengetriebene Prozessoptimierung aktiv mit. Du setzt dein theoretisches Wissen aus dem Studium – etwa aus Methoden- und Projektmanagement – direkt in die Praxis um und sammelst dabei wertvolle Erfahrungen. Wir bieten dir ein Umfeld, das dir Sicherheit und Selbstvertrauen für deinen Berufseinstieg gibt. Flexible Arbeitsbedingungen und ein engagiertes, unterstützendes Team begleiten dich auf deinem Weg zum erfolgreichen Bachelorabschluss. Hinweis: Das Praktikum ist ausschliesslich in Kombination mit einem praxisintegrierten Bachelorstudium an der Fachhochschule Nordwestschweiz FHNW \/ Zürcher Hochschule für angewandte Wissenschaften ZHAW möglich. Weitere Informationen findest du hier: Link zu FHNW: https:\/\/www.fhnw.ch\/de\/studium\/technik\/pibs Link zu ZHAW: https:\/\/www.zhaw.ch\/de\/engineering\/studium\/bachelorstudium\/praxisintegriertes-bachelorstudium\/ Das kannst du bewirken Automatisiere Geschäftsprozesse mit Power BI, Power Automate oder Power Platform Betreue und begleite sowohl bestehende als auch neue Projekte Arbeite mit Manufacturing Equipment Systems (MES) und Datenbanken Erstelle Prozessabläufe nach BPMN 2.0 und treibe die Digitalisierung (Process Mining, Citizen Development und Businessanalysen) voran Verbinde technisches Know-how mit betriebswirtschaftlichem Denken und setze die Theorie in Praxis um (Requirement Engineering) Lerne bei uns wie man verschiedene Anspruchsgruppen abholt um erfolgreich Wirkung zu erzielen (Change Management) Das bringst du mit Abgeschlossene Gymnasiale Matura (Voraussetzungen für die Zulassung an der Fachhochschule FHNW\/ZHAW) Offenheit, Proaktivität und Selbstmotivation Ausgeprägte Kommunikationsfähigkeit und soziale Kompetenz Fähigkeit zur eigenständigen und strukturierten Arbeitsweise Fliessendes Deutsch (mindestens C1), Kenntnisse in Englisch (B1) Mehr über uns Unsere flexiblen Arbeitspraktiken helfen dir, persönliche und berufliche Ziele zu verbinden. Wir bieten dir ein Umfeld, in dem du deine Fähigkeiten entwickeln und wachsen kannst – unterstützt durch Mentoring und eine inklusive Kultur. Haben wir dein Interesse Geweckt? Dann freuen wir uns auf deine Bewerbung inklusive CV, Motivationsschreiben und Zeugnisse.

Weitere Informationen findest du hier: Link zu FHNW: https:\/\/www.fhnw.ch\/de\/studium\/technik\/pibs Link zu ZHAW: https:\/\/www.zhaw.ch\/de\/engineering\/studium\/bachelorstudium\/praxisintegriertes-bachelorstudium\/ Das kannst du bewirken Automatisiere Geschäftsprozesse mit Power BI, Power Automate oder Power Platform Betreue und begleite sowohl bestehende als auch neue Projekte Arbeite mit Manufacturing Equipment Systems (MES) und Datenbanken Erstelle Prozessabläufe nach BPMN 2.0 und treibe die Digitalisierung (Process Mining, Citizen Development und Businessanalysen) voran Verbinde technisches Know-how mit betriebswirtschaftlichem Denken und setze die Theorie in Praxis um (Requirement Engineering) Lerne bei uns wie man verschiedene Anspruchsgruppen abholt um erfolgreich Wirkung zu erzielen (Change Management) Das bringst du mit Abgeschlossene Gymnasiale Matura (Voraussetzungen für die Zulassung an der Fachhochschule FHNW\/ZHAW) Offenheit, Proaktivität und Selbstmotivation Ausgeprägte Kommunikationsfähigkeit und soziale Kompetenz Fähigkeit zur eigenständigen und strukturierten Arbeitsweise Fliessendes Deutsch (mindestens C1), Kenntnisse in Englisch (B1) Mehr über uns Unsere flexiblen Arbeitspraktiken helfen dir, persönliche und berufliche Ziele zu verbinden. Wir bieten dir ein Umfeld, in dem du deine Fähigkeiten entwickeln und wachsen kannst – unterstützt durch Mentoring und eine inklusive Kultur. Haben wir dein Interesse Geweckt? Dann freuen wir uns auf deine Bewerbung inklusive CV, Motivationsschreiben und Zeugnisse.
